LanguageData.AA_Bullet_TinyVenomBarb.label: tiny venomous barb
LanguageData.AA_ExplodingWeb.label: exploding web
LanguageData.AA_HugeQuill.label: huge quill
LanguageData.GR_AberrantFleshbeast.description: Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. Whilst it doesn’t pose any threat and will slowly bleed to death, attacking it can trigger its self-defense instincts.
LanguageData.GR_AberrantFleshbeast.label: aberrant fleshbeast
LanguageData.GR_AberrantFleshbeast.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenetic.description: A payload of alpha animal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an alpha genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenetic.label: alpha genome
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_AlphaGenomeExcavator.label: alpha genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_AncientBioBattery.description: A very efficient battery that has somehow managed to survive for so long without maintenance, purpose-built to power genome storage rooms.
LanguageData.GR_AncientBioBattery.label: ancient microbiobattery
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrate.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrate.label: ancient ruined crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrateDouble.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCrateDouble.label: ancient ruined crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientCratePile.description: Whatever these crates contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCratePile.label: pile of ancient ruined crates
LanguageData.GR_AncientCryofreezer.description: A super-charged air-conditioning unit designed for its intended purpose of preventing genomes from breaking down entropically. Unfortunately, that temperature is also beyond the comfort range of almost all organic life but it can no longer maintain the desired output of approaching absolute zero.
LanguageData.GR_AncientCryofreezer.label: ancient cryofreezer
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowomb.description: This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowomb.label: ancient electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombLarge.description: The exterior shell has completely fallen apart, yet it seems that it exploded instead of imploded.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombLarge.label: ancient large electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombSpawner.description: This device has collapsed in on itself, rendering it useless.
LanguageData.GR_AncientElectrowombSpawner.label: ancient electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_AncientGeneticsTinkeringTable.descrip: Most of the useful and otherwise functional parts are missing, indicating intentional sabotage.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGeneticsTinkeringTable.label: ancient genetics tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeExtractor.description: Lacking proper maintenance for so long, this machine is rusted shut.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeExtractor.label: ancient genome extractor table
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeRecombinator.description: A very old recombinator that somehow possesses an awful stench and excessive mould.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomeRecombinator.label: ancient genome recombinator
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomorpher.description: A Genomorpher that has fallen into complete disrepair, some salvage still remains.
LanguageData.GR_AncientGenomorpher.label: ancient genomorpher
LanguageData.GR_AncientTechCrate.description: Whatever this crate contained has long ago rotted and turned to dust.
LanguageData.GR_AncientTechCrate.label: ancient mechanized crate
LanguageData.GR_AncientTissueGrinder.description: The machinery and electronics are irreparable but could be broken down for scrap.
LanguageData.GR_AncientTissueGrinder.label: ancient tissue grinder
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.description: The Animus Hare is a small leporid with latent psionic abilities. Quite intelligent for an animal, but not intelligent enough to properly use psionics. It will however quickly bond with human beings and reach with his mind to make happy everyone that treats it well.\n\nUseful abilities: they will often use a psionic nuzzle on colonists, keeping everyone happier. They also breed very fast.
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.label: animus hare
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_AnimusHare.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ArchocentipedeFormer.description:
The archotech version of the Genomorpher, this machine combines the best genetic material of all the animal branches into a single Archocentipede Growthcell. The way it functions is beyond understanding and is impossible to replicate even with the best glitterworld technology.

In order for this machine to function, it requires multiple connecting DNA Storage Banks which each have the complete genetic data from the different animal branches. Each storage bank will be completely drained in order to ensure that the resulting Growthcell is perfect in every single way.

The resulting Archocentipede Growthcell requires its own special Archowomb, as it is impossible to replicate the necessary nurturing environment.

Genomes needed:
LanguageData.GR_ArchocentipedeFormer.label: archocentipede former
LanguageData.GR_ArchoGrowthcell.description: A portable, archotech-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. This particular one is completely stable and contains the embryo of an Archocentipede, which can only grow in an Archowomb.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born.
LanguageData.GR_ArchoGrowthcell.label: archotech growth cell
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.description:
The Archocentipede is the end result of the Archotech Project, aiming to provide a "friendly" machine superintelligence with a capable body. Even though this centipede is forcefully loyal to the colonists, and can be somewhat controlled by them, most other inhabitants of the planet will consider its existence to be an abomination, since dabbling in archotech usually signals the end of comprehensible human life.

Useful abilities: What do you mean useful? You have WON the game. The Archocentipede is basically unkillable by normal means, just use it to lay waste to everything on the planet. You don't have enemies now, you have pets. Ooooor... the archotech may kill all your colonists. One of the two.

Based on concept art for the Nydus Worm by Federico Dallocchio from Blizzard Entertainment at the Starcraft wiki, https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Nydus_worm/Development.
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.label: archocentipede
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.verbs.0.label: disintegrator
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechCentipede.verbs.1.label: twin-coiled laser
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechGenomeExcavator.description:
An advanced genome extraction device that is capable of self-arming and self-guiding at the touch of a button. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome. Each device holds up to three charges before it becomes useless.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is guaranteed to be painless and instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body... The device is much better at compressing material and as such can be used on even the largest of organisms.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_ArchotechGenomeExcavator.label: archotech genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_ArchoWomb.description: A relatively massive machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb specifically for the Archocentipede. Once the Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released.\n\nCare should be taken before beginning the process, as the resulting energy signature will force any and all hostiles to attack the colony before the monstrosity can be born. The procedure will last 15 days and once finished, the creature will be under the player’s control with peerless combat prowess.
LanguageData.GR_ArchoWomb.label: archowomb
LanguageData.GR_AvianGenetic.description:
A payload of avian genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary avian hybrids lay eggs. Combat avian hybrids get increased movement speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_AvianGenetic.label: avian genome
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.description:
An unusual concoction of traits that was favoured by a particular client who was obsessed on making things explode. Earned the nickname Boom-Boom as they would often make requests along the lines of “two more booms, wasn’t paying attention”.

An ursine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Lacking intelligence compared to a bear while also missing out on some strength and confidence in hunting. However, they can be commanded to detonate a limited amount of fuel sacks for increased combat versatility in exchange for hurting themselves intentionally.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. The "Detonate chemfuel" ability can be used to cause a powerful "Bomb" damage type explosion surrounding the hybrid. Bear in mind (pun intended) that this will damage the hybrid too!
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.label: bearalope
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearalope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.description: This tardigrade-like creature is basically a huge bed bug infused with bear genes. It consumes a large amount of food, but its paralysing attacks combined with its resilience can be very useful in a combat situation.\n\nUseful abilities: Bear bugs produce a powerful neurotoxin that can paralyse their prey, leaving them completely unable to defend themselves.
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.label: bear bug
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_BearBug.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggFertilized.description: Fertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way... If all goes well, it should hatch into a bear bug.
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggFertilized.label: bear bug egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized bear bug egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Actually, it's a beetle egg, it is disgusting either way...
LanguageData.GR_BearBugEggUnfertilized.label: bear bug egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.description:
Massive, fluffy murder babies is just one of the many “cute” nicknames made for this animal, their only downside being that they’re unable to purr. Because they’re so large, they often end up knocking over the people they’re trying to show affection to before laying on top of them for warmth.

An ursine hybrid with recessive feline genes. Retaining their intelligence and being faster and more fierce in combat, they are adorable killing machines that love to nuzzle. They prefer easier prey and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, you monster.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. The pelt can be sold or used in the art workbench to create rugs.
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.label: bearcat
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearcat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.description:
A surprisingly clean animal dubbed the “owl-bear” by those that find them amusing as they attempt to fly by rapidly flapping their stubby vestigial wings. That is until they get their eyes pecked out and flattened by their weight.

An ursine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Slightly dumber but easier to train, they lay eggs every couple of days and have the strength to protect their clutch. Slower than a bear but their ability to hover over terrain means they perform better in harsh terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Although too heavy to fly, it can use its wings to nullify terrain movement costs.
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.label: bearchicken
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearchicken.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggFertilized.description: Fertilized bearchicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggFertilized.label: bearchicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized bearchicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BearChickenEggUnfertilized.label: bearchicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_BearClaws.description: An improvement over the bionic Power Claw, these claws have been vat-grown with a mixture of human and bear DNA, and replace the subject's hand with versatile, non-retractable claws.
LanguageData.GR_BearClaws.label: human Implant: bear claws
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.description:
A wonderful animal for those cold winter journeys, very popular as travellers snuggle into their fur at night to keep warm. Fights have even broken out over who gets to sleep with the oversized rug.

An ursine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Very filthy but a decently intelligent pack animal that produces good-quality wool and milk. Performs very well in cold environments and is more than capable of defending itself.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.label: bearffalo
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearffalo.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearffaloMilk.description: Milk from a bearffalo. Quite high in fat content, and very nutritive.
LanguageData.GR_BearffaloMilk.label: bearffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_Bearhorse.description:
While they’re surprisingly tireless when they have a home to rest at, these manned mares are quite reluctant to hurry along when made to carry loads in a caravan. Their body’s efficiency means they’ll get a task done when it needs to, and not a moment sooner. What makes it worse is that they’re smart enough to know that they’re cute - and powerful - enough to get away with it.

An equine hybrid with recessive ursine genes. The inherent hibernating nature of bears has combined with the high energy of horses to create this particular hybrid that requires little in the way of rest and a much more efficient and versatile digestive system. They are quite a bit slower as a consequence, along with their higher body mass.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Bearhorse.label: bearhorse
LanguageData.GR_Bearhorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearhorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Bearhorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_BearLeg.description: A prosthetic animal leg strengthened with bear muscles, and ending in sharp bear claws that add a powerful melee attack.
LanguageData.GR_BearLeg.label: animal Implant: bear leg
LanguageData.GR_BearLiver.description: An implant that uses bear genes to improve function of the liver. Specially useful for those alcoholic chickens.
LanguageData.GR_BearLiver.label: animal Implant: bear liver
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.description:
A terrifying combination of the raw power of a bear and the intellect of a human, resulting in a creature that loves to abuse its prey. Not quite as strong due to the strange hand-paw shapes, but very energetic and eager to get their victims to catch these hands.

An ursine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very high intellect but comparatively fragile compared to similar hybrids. Their bodies contain oversized and overstimulated adrenal glands that are found just under the surface of the skin, which can be regularly extracted for a substance analogous to Go-Juice.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.label: bearman
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.description:
Not many people get to see a naked bear, and fewer still want a repeat viewing. This oversized mole-rat inspires plenty of fear with its massive teeth, but gets a lot of love and affection from those wanting a large, hairless pet that gnaws on the furniture.

An ursine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Very slow both mentally and physically along with dietary restrictions, but very fierce in combat and immune to pain. Also they have the added benefit of having a short reproductive cycle, low metabolism and being completely fearless in hunting prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. No pain sensitivity. This hybrid won't be knocked down in combat due to pain.
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.label: bearmole
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearmole.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BearMuscleTissue.description: Grafts of bear muscle tissue on the subject's torso increase overall strength and stamina. These grafts however tend to stimulate the production of an abundance of body hair.
LanguageData.GR_BearMuscleTissue.label: human Implant: bear muscle tissue
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.description:
The dinosaur nerds out there should love this hybrid. Got one hell of a nasty bite and even the claws will both knock you down and make you unable to get up. Often bred for hunting by the bored one percenters who want to eat massive drumsticks, caveman style.

A reptile hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Highly intelligent and an exceptional frontline trooper, with a venomous, stunning attack, fast movement speed and high health pool.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.label: bearodile
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearodile.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearscarab.description:
An absolute unit of an insectoid hybrid, this massive chubster is incredibly smart and hard to kill. All the excess blubber has been replaced with either chitin or muscle fibre, so don’t go making comments about their bulk.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Very thick armour plating and a calm temperament make this hybrid an excellent combat animal. Requires little in the way of retraining and can dish out respectable damage as well.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Bearscarab.label: bearscarab
LanguageData.GR_Bearscarab.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.description:
Great care must be taken before considering raising this chonky boy, as they have a metabolism that matches their high ferocity and movement speed. Pretty useful in keeping egg-laying animal populations in check.

An ursine hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very fierce and intelligent combat hybrid with an incredible appetite. As they are almost constantly hungry, they will dig for berries when they’re hungry provided it’s growing season.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.label: bearwolf
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Bearwolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.description:
An incredibly quick - mentally and physically - hunter with an overactive castoreum gland, effectively allowing them to actively convert meat and trees into vegetables. Technically vegans should be fine with it since, much like bees, it needs to be harvested and the beaverwolf will absolutely let you know if it doesn’t like you touching it.

A rodent hybrid with recessive canine genes. A very quick but cautious predator, this hybrid is both intelligent and frequently looking for its next meal. Able to freely roam and doesn’t require a pen, they are both carnivorous and dendrovorous while also offering daily harvests of castoreum which functions as a vegetable in dishes.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.label: beaverwolf
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Beaverwolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BeeGenetic.description:
A payload of bee genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used only as a primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bee hybrids hover over terrain.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table from a combination of 10 bees.
LanguageData.GR_BeeGenetic.label: bee genome
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.description: A Bear / Bee hybrid. This huge bee hybrid is fiercely agressive and territorial. It will act as a very powerful attack animal with venomous attacks if properly trained.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.label: bearbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Bearbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.description: A Boomalope / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in chemfuel pustules and it is highly unstable.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The boombee injects chemfuel with its stinger, instead of venom, which is as painful as it sounds. It will also explode in a cloud of bees when it dies, which is incredibly stupid from a scientific standpoint, but it is cool, so meh. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.label: boombee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Boombee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.description: A Cat / Bee hybrid. The agile catbee is an affectionate pet that will also sting your enemies to death.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The catbee has a powerful venomous sting that makes its prey blind for a while. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.label: catbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Catbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.description: A Chicken / Bee hybrid. As it is often the case with avian hybrids, they are not very strong on their own, but when used in high enough numbers they can be a real nuisance.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The chickenbee is an excellent egg layer. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.label: chickenbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Chickenbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.description: A Lizard / Bee hybrid. This bee hybrid is covered in scales, providing it with a thick natural armor.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. Lizardbees have a very potent venom. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.label: lizardbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Lizardbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.description: A Mouse / Bee hybrid. These diminutive bee hybrids reproduce really fast.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The mousebee reproduces with blinding speed. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.label: mousebee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Mousebee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.description: A Muffalo / Bee hybrid. This docile bee hybrid is a versatile pack beast, since it ignores terrain movement costs. Apart from its honeycombs, it also produces additional small amounts of milk and wool.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.label: muffalobee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Muffalobee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.description: A Megaspider / Bee hybrid. A powerful, armoured bee hybrid that cannot reproduce on its own. It has a dangerous ranged attack.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting, and a ranged sting attack. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.label: spiderbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Spiderbee.tools.2.label: slicing mandibles
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.description: A Thrumbo / Bee hybrid. This gigantic bee hybrid is very intelligent, and it can easily be the backbone of a colony's defense if trained to do it.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. They also have a very nasty venomous sting. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.label: thrumbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Thrumbee.tools.2.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.description: A Wolf / Bee hybrid. A fearsome flying predator. One of the fastest creatures on the Rim, due to its ability to ignore rugged terrain. Perfect for chasing down intruders.\n\nUseful abilities: All bee hybrids slowly produce their appropriate honeycombs every few days. The wolfbee has a powerful venomous sting that paralyzes its prey. Finally, they will ignore terrain movement costs, since they hover over the ground, except over water, where their movement is slightly impeded.
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.label: wolfbee
LanguageData.GR_BeeHybrid_Wolfbee.tools.1.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.description: A chitinous, floating, hydrogen-filled monstruosity. The beetlefleet wanders the land bouncing off objects in its path, seeking prey to consume.\n\nUseful abilities: Beetlefleet produce insect jelly, explode on death violently, and reproduce asexually.
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.label: beetlefleet
LanguageData.GR_Beetlefleet.tools.1.label: tentacles
LanguageData.GR_BioLight.description: A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.
LanguageData.GR_BioLight.label: ancient bioluminescent light
LanguageData.GR_BiomechanicalLabBeacon.description: This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. The radio waves it is constantly emitting are being decoded, but the process seems to have destabilized its power source, and it will blow up soon!
LanguageData.GR_BiomechanicalLabBeacon.label: biomechanical lab beacon
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.description: Originally intended as a way to make boomalopes more combat viable, their powerful muscles have instead been focused on compressing its produced chemfuel as a way to store greater amounts of chemfuel without care of how dangerous it is.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Consumes more than twice as much compared to a boomalope but produces deepchem instead. They’re also capable of defending themselves should any predator be foolish enough to go after them.
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.label: boomabear
LanguageData.GR_Boomabear.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.description: A biological weapon in compact form, these living, breathing, shitting bombs are ideally assembled into a massive horde and sent squawking at the enemy. Allegedly, this particular hybrid has been brought up in certain court cases as the original creator thought it would be funny to create “lethal egg timers”.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive avian genes. Produces edible eggs very slowly, but can lay a clutch of explosive eggs twice a day. They’re both reasonably intelligent and quick on their feet.
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.label: boomachicken
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomachicken.tools.1.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_BoomachickenEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Boomachicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BoomachickenEggFertilized.label: boomachicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_BoomachickenEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized Boomachicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_BoomachickenEggUnfertilized.label: boomachicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.description: The boomalisk is a boomalope / dunealisk hybrid that produces a weird spider silk know as boomthread. This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator. Boomalisks also catch their prey with exploding webs, which... sounds dangerous.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces chemfuel, boomthread and attacks with explosive webs.
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.label: boomalisk
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.0.label: left first leg
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.1.label: right first leg
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomalisk.verbs.0.label: exploding web
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeGenetic.description:
A payload of boom genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All boom hybrids explode on death. Farm hybrids produce chemfuel, while combat hybrids can use exploding abilities.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeGenetic.label: boom genome
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeHeart.description: An implant that uses chemfuel to pump blood through the animal's body. This implant is intended for creatures whose heart is failing catastrophically, since it is less efficient than a natural heart.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeHeart.label: animal Implant: boomalope heart
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeNodules.description: These skin nodules can be integrated in a human's torso, causing a huge explosion if the subjects dies violently.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeNodules.label: human Implant: boomalope nodules
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeStomach.description: An implant that uses boomalope genes to improve function of the stomach.
LanguageData.GR_BoomalopeStomach.label: animal Implant: boomalope stomach
LanguageData.GR_Booman.description:
Outright refuses to let anyone else milk them for their chemfuel as they view it as a violation of their humanoid rights. They make for a good conversation partner even if their stance can be a little volatile.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Instead of supplying a regular amount of chemfuel, this hybrid will syphon themselves to top up a limited number of machinery powered by chemfuel, such as generators and pod launchers.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Boomen periodically refuel some machines powered by chemfuel in a 10 tile radius (chemfuel generators and pod launchers).
LanguageData.GR_Booman.label: booman
LanguageData.GR_Booman.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boombeetle.description:
For lovers of xenomorphs everywhere, this bomb-ass bombadier is ready to launch sacs of acidic chemfuel at your enemies for fun and profit. Don’t expect a second pair of jaws though, that mouth is for spitting only.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Quite temperamental for a hybrid, but they prove to be worth it as they store caustic chemfuel in their sacs which can then be lobbed at a distance. Practically useless in melee, so keep them safe and provide fire support. Can be ordered to detonate auxiliary acid sacs as a defensive measure, but that will also self-harm.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Boombeetle.label: boombeetle
LanguageData.GR_Boombeetle.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boombeetle.verbs.0.label: explosive sac
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.description:
Combining the frequent nuzzling of a cat and the deepchem production of a primary boomalope hybrid is surely in no way going to end badly. Better not to ignore them should they complain loudly about wanting dinner.

A boomalope hybrid with recessive feline genes. They make for an interesting companion for those who desire chemfuel production but also want to be able to cuddle said creatures. They can also be butchered to retrieve an internal gland filled with deepchem.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.label: boomcat
LanguageData.GR_Boomcat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.description: People love efficiency and having one thing to cover all their needs. Well this isn’t it, but it gets damn close. The chemfuel sacs have mixed with milk-producing glands, which make for a milk that smells like pure gasoline and could almost power a vehicle.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Has a large appetite due to it producing multiple products, namely deepchem, thin wool and an unusual milk that shares properties with Chemshine.
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.label: boomffalo
LanguageData.GR_Boomffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomhorse.description:
Inherently less effective at refining chemfuel when allowed to rest at its leisure while also being a good pack animal and riding companion. During distant travels, the long periods of engagement for their muscles actively puts their glands into overdrive. Quite effective should there be a vehicle that uses chemfuel, but don’t ask how that would work out. Maybe it’s the desire to compete against machinery or knowing that if the vehicle breaks down it's gonna have to do all that bloody walking again.

An equine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. While they are comparatively slower, their inherent need to exercise means that their capability in producing chemfuel is severely hampered if their muscles aren’t engaged for long periods of time. When travelling as part of a caravan, their chemfuel production speed is effectively quadrupled.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Boomhorse.label: boomhorse
LanguageData.GR_Boomhorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomhorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Boomhorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.description: The Boomnotherium is an hybrid between a boomalope and a large prehistoric relative of modern-day elephants. In latin, boomnotherium roughly translates as "terrible beast that will explode in your ass".\n\nUseful abilities: Boomnotheriums produce huge amounts of chemfuel, and they are "tanky", able to withstand a lot of abuse before exploding.
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.label: boomnotherium
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.tools.0.label: tusk
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.tools.1.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.tools.2.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Boomnotherium.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.description:
What happens when you mix a spitting cobra, a rattlesnake and a creature designed to lactate refined fuel? This abomination, that’s what. Drive it into a corner and it’ll hurt itself to release deadly neurotoxins. Try and keep your distance and it’ll spit toxic blood right at you. Clearly someone was a sore loser in playing pretend games as a child, but had either the brains or the money to prove themselves right.

A reptile hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Decently intelligent with a nasty bite and even nastier ranged attack, both causing toxic buildup. Quite a high metabolism which supports its various combat abilities, best suited for home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.label: boomsnake
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomsnake.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.description: With adorable horns and long snoots, these squeaky lads are ideal in giving your enemy a stunning surprise. Their chemfuel mixture includes other gases that create a blinding and disorienting flashbang when remotely detonated. They’ll still make a regular boom if killed though.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite fast and intelligent, these hybrids work as excellent swarm attackers. Their rapid reproduction cycle and stunning explosion make for an ideal companion to ranged masters.
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.label: boomsquirrel
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Boomsquirrel.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Boomthread.description: This silk is as strong as synthread, but it is also an excellent thermal insulator
LanguageData.GR_Boomthread.label: boomthread
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.description: For those who wished they could equip their canines with explosive vests, the Boomwolf stores pressurised - almost solidified - chemfuel which can be remotely discharged as a non-explosive gas. Thanks to thermodynamics, it is extremely cold upon release and can just as easily hurt the hybrid as well as everything nearby.\n\nA boomalope hybrid with recessive canine genes. Very intelligent, fast and dangerous, they make for an interesting combat animal. One of the few canine-type hybrids that work better solo than as a pack.
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.label: boomwolf
LanguageData.GR_Boomwolf.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_BoosterController.description: Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted but increases the chance of failure due to introduction of invasive foreign material.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterController.label: controller
LanguageData.GR_BoosterFertility.description: Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.\n\nNote: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterFertility.label: fertility unblocker
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmono.description: Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Effectively reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmono.label: harmonobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmonoGreater.description: Capsules containing genetic stabilizers that help maintain the dominant-recessive genome relationship. Greatly reduces the chance of the hybrid having the opposite intended result, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterHarmonoGreater.label: improved harmonobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmuno.description: Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Effectively helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmuno.label: immunobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmunoGreater.description: Capsules containing genetic restorative medicine that helps prevent genomes from breaking down prematurely. Greatly helps mitigate the possibility of the hybrid ending in complete failure, at the expense of more processing time.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterImmunoGreater.label: improved immunobooster
LanguageData.GR_BoosterStabilizator.description: Compressed medicine that slows the growth of the specimen, increasing both stability and safety. Effectively increases growth time by 50% in exchange for a vastly increased chance of obtaining the intended result.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterStabilizator.label: stabilizator
LanguageData.GR_BoosterTempo.description: Capsules containing growth hormones that help speed up the hybrid’s growth process, halving the time it takes to complete.
LanguageData.GR_BoosterTempo.label: tempobooster
LanguageData.GR_BunnyEars.description: This huge leporid ear gives amazing enhanced hearing. Also, super kawaii!
LanguageData.GR_BunnyEars.label: human Implant: bunny ear
LanguageData.GR_CanidEye.description: An eye implant that uses genetic splicing with canid genes to give an old (or damaged) eye decent vision. This implant is not meant to replace healthy eyes.
LanguageData.GR_CanidEye.label: animal Implant: canid eye
LanguageData.GR_CanineGenetic.description:
A payload of canine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary canine hybrids have the adrenaline rush ability. All canine hybrids have fast attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_CanineGenetic.label: canine genome
LanguageData.GR_Castoreum.description: Castoreum is a yellowish exudate from the castor sacs of beavers. It is historically used as a food additive and natural flavour, often as a substitute vanilla flavour.
LanguageData.GR_Castoreum.label: beaverwolf castoreum
LanguageData.GR_Catalope.description:
Have you ever wanted to have a cat that could start fires with hairballs? No? Too bad, this one does and if you’re unlucky it’ll be outside when it’s hunting. Which won’t be often, because those fuel sacs make for a surprisingly efficient energy reserve. Exploding kittens is no longer just a game, it’s a hazard warning.

A feline hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Intelligent, low maintenance with a weak ranged flame attack, they make for an unusual companion. 

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight.
LanguageData.GR_Catalope.label: catalope
LanguageData.GR_Catalope.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Catalope.verbs.0.label: chemfuel spit
LanguageData.GR_Catbear.description:
The perfect companion in compact form, this tiny terror is willing to throw down with its surprising bulk. And good lord, what an affectionate beefcake.

A feline hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Can both take and dish out punishment disproportionate to its size while being clever enough to actively hunt for prey not much larger than itself. Incredibly easy to train and will intermittently dig up burrowed rats.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Stunning attacks like bear hybrids.
LanguageData.GR_Catbear.label: catbear
LanguageData.GR_Catbear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Catbear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Catbear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Catchicken.description:
This smart little shit knows where to kick so it hurts, and that’s often the eyes. The unusual genetic makeup has made their claws as long as a human thumb, so best to pet it when it wants to and avoid touching the belly.

A feline hybrid with recessive avian genes. A little slow for its size, it makes for a vicious attack animal if it can be permitted to get in melee range. Be careful as it can’t take much punishment.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Go for the eyes: Catchicken attacks have a 25% chance to affect the victim's eyes.
LanguageData.GR_Catchicken.label: catchicken
LanguageData.GR_Catchicken.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_CatEye.description: This hybrid humanoid / feline eye provides improved night vision, at the expense of a certain short-sightedness outside and during the day. Ideal for nyctophile colonists.
LanguageData.GR_CatEye.label: human Implant: cat eye
LanguageData.GR_Catffalo.description:
Someone couldn’t bear being separated from their trusty feline companions when away on trips. So they decided to make one that everyone would want to take with them on long journeys. Also they look incredibly adorable with their little baggies.

A feline hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. While not great as a pack animal, they make for a fantastic caravaning companion as they will actively forage for prey during trips. This creates a stacking effect of boosting the amount of food foraged for every other catffalo in the trip. They’re quite hardy too but should be kept away from combat.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Forages for small prey while caravaning, increasing the amount of foraged food by 10% (and it stacks per catffalo on the caravan!).
LanguageData.GR_Catffalo.label: catffalo
LanguageData.GR_Catffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Catffalo.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Catffalo.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Cathorse.description:
Tell me, do you want a cat large enough to ride at speeds that would be illegal on most urbworld city streets? Of course you do, and if you say otherwise you’re a coward. This ferocious feline is ready to tear up the track and hunt prey comically smaller than itself. They’re also incredibly smart and have a tendency to stalk their owners when their backs are turned. Don’t worry though, it only wants to pin you down and drown you in fur and saliva.

An equine hybrid with recessive feline genes. Quite durable and combat capable for a pack animal, they can be drafted to execute at-will pouncing to close distance with the enemy. They are only slightly less effective as horses in riding but have better stamina, in exchange they are pure carnivores so their diet can be rather restrictive.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, making them perfect for the front lines, and a short nuzzle period. Always starts as draftable, even if not using a controller booster to create it. Pounce ability: when drafted, the player can use the pounce ability to make the hybrid jump around. The ability can be triggered every 2 hours.
LanguageData.GR_Cathorse.label: cathorse
LanguageData.GR_Cathorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Cathorse.tools.1.label: left hoof-paw
LanguageData.GR_Cathorse.tools.2.label: right hoof-paw
LanguageData.GR_Cathulhu.description:
You seriously have to wonder who comes up with ideas to play with things beyond their understanding. Have you ever had an octopus attach itself to your face and try to climb down your throat? Well now you can find out, with an extra helping of teeth, claws and furballs.

A feline hybrid with recessive Eldritch genes. Comparable to a regular big cat, such as a panther, however its bite not only causes blindness but also inflicts bouts of insanity from the lingering touch of the tentacles. Oddly enough it also enjoys fondling its master with those same tentacles, notably without its teeth out. Oh, did I not mention the fact it has teeth along those tentacles?

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Its bites cause suffocation and sanity loss.
LanguageData.GR_Cathulhu.label: cathulhu
LanguageData.GR_Cathulhu.tools.0.label: rending tentacles
LanguageData.GR_Cathulhu.tools.1.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Cathulhu.tools.2.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Catman.description:
Just the sweetest little kitty cat you’ll ever encounter, they were bred as tiny caretakers to look after people in hospital wards. Despite being a health violation, they were extremely popular with the infirms as they not only fed patients but also engaged in conversation and gave friendly headbutts. It is also alleged that their purring helped broken bones heal quicker, but no conclusive evidence has been shown.

A feline hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Quite slow and fragile, comparatively speaking, with a low damage output and lower hunger rate. However, they make for very good bedside companions for the ill and will regularly feed those that cannot feed themselves.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Catmen are compassionate animals that will help with feeding medical patients around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Catman.label: catman
LanguageData.GR_Catman.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Catrabbit.description:
Everyone can agree that cats and rabbits have incredibly soft fur, so can you imagine how cute and fluffy they would be if you mixed their traits together? They also love to be pet multiple times a day, so everyone can get in on this pet so fluffy it's to die for.

A feline hybrid with recessive rodent genes. While a somewhat independent carnivore, this hybrid is ideal inside a colony as it not only provides a non-stacking mood boost to those nearby from its cuteness, but it also nuzzles every few hours. They’re also very fast breeders.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Gives non-stacking +3 mood to everyone in a 8 tiles radius.
LanguageData.GR_Catrabbit.label: catrabbit
LanguageData.GR_Catrabbit.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Catrabbit.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Catrabbit.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_CatTail.description: A tail implant that uses genetic splicing with feline genes to substitute a broken or missing tail. This implant is not meant to replace a healthy tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_CatTail.label: animal Implant: cat tail
LanguageData.GR_Catwolf.description:
For some reason people really want cats to be able to carry stuff, depicted often in consumer media. Well this palico should have you sorted, however despite numerous efforts it has proved impossible to train them in using brooms.

A feline hybrid with recessive canine genes. An incredibly fast hauling companion that can also defend itself, these creatures borne forth from pop culture are incredibly smart and incredibly clean. They can’t carry much, but having a whole army won’t cause your base to fill with filth, just the normal degenerates.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability, which in the case of these small animals is ideal to get out of a fight. Produces (and spreads) absolutely no filth.
LanguageData.GR_Catwolf.label: catwolf
LanguageData.GR_Catwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Catwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Catwolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelHeart.description: A reinforced bionic heart that acts like a miniature 4-stroke internal combustion engine powered by chemfuel to pump blood through the body.
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelHeart.label: human Implant: chemfuel heart
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelStomach.description: A reinforced bionic stomach that uses a combination of gastric acid and chemfuel to efficiently digest food.
LanguageData.GR_ChemfuelStomach.label: human Implant: chemfuel stomach
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.description:
For something so small, this animal has a hell of an appetite. Lacking in many departments, but the nutrition has to go somewhere and that’s for those looking for a non-vegan friendly option of getting high. The person who thought it would be a good idea to inhale the burning feathers of this plump creature is one stoners everywhere praise while they blaze.

An avian hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Has a considerably larger appetite and inherent wildness, but reproduces quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Can be regularly plucked for chickenbear feathers. When these feathers are turned into powder at a Drug lab, they can be rolled into a joint to be smoked.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.label: chickenbear
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbear.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenbearEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickenbear egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenbearEggFertilized.label: chickenbear egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenbearEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized Chickenbear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenbearEggUnfertilized.label: chickenbear egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbearfeathers.description: Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder called featherdust, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenbearfeathers.label: chickenbear feathers
LanguageData.GR_ChickenBrains.description: An implant that uses chicken brain tissue to patch damaged neurological pathways. This implant is intended for brain-dead creatures.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenBrains.label: animal Implant: chicken brains
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.description:
Respectably intelligent as well as lethal, these egg-laying, plump beasties love nuzzling their owners and biting their enemies. Sometimes those are different people.

An avian hybrid with recessive feline genes. Quite clean with a low gestation period, they make for an average farm animal and an average combatant with good speed. They still require a pen, so they make for good home defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.label: chickencat
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickencat.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickencatEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickencat egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickencatEggFertilized.label: chickencat egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickencatEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized Chickencat egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickencatEggUnfertilized.label: chickencat egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.description: You wouldn’t think something so small could eat so much, but that’s the price you pay for the sake of convenience. That convenience is sporadic, low in nutrition and very thin, an effect commonly described as “nerfing”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Loves to wander so it must be securely penned, but they make for a surprising pack beast. Capable of producing the holy trinity of milk, eggs and wool, however they can be quite lacking in substance.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.label: chickenffalo
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenffalo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickenffalo egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloEggFertilized.label: chickenffalo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized Chickenffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloEggUnfertilized.label: chickenffalo egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloMilk.description: Milk from a chickenffalo. Looks like watered down milk. Probably not much better than water.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenffaloMilk.label: chickenffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_Chickenhorse.description:
An agile avian that most people expect to be more yellow in colour and go “wark” every now and then. A delightfully speedy mount that can pull off some impressive turns, this hybrid has been bred even further with other species to make for more colourful and genetically stable bird mounts. Some speculate that this particular creature is the original ancestor of the Ave, while the Royal Ave came from those that were bred more for plumage than performance.

An equine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Comparatively smaller than a purebred, but incredibly fast both in a singular location and as part of a caravan. A rather unusual hybrid in that it reproduces very slowly in a safe, similar environment, however this changes on caravan trips. Constant new sights and experiences puts their reproductive cycle into overdrive as it tries to spread its species over a broad horizon, however these eggs are genetically sterile so that they instead provide a healthy protein supplement for those who can only gather fruits and vegetables.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenhorse.label: chickenhorse
LanguageData.GR_Chickenhorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenhorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Chickenhorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_ChickenhorseEggFertilized.description: Fertilized chickenhorse egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenhorseEggFertilized.label: chickenhorse egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenhorseEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized chickenhorse egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenhorseEggUnfertilized.label: chickenhorse egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenKidney.description: An implant that uses chicken genes to improve function of the kidney. Birds have large kidneys. Yep, you learned that today.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenKidney.label: animal Implant: chicken kidney
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.description:
Chickens are basically an evolution of dinosaurs, so someone decided to turn them back. Unfortunately they didn’t exactly bother to check that they should still be avian rather than reptilian, so it resulted in this nasty little thing that reproduces at an insane rate and empties your fridge. But hey, they’ll spit on your enemies and their right to shit everywhere, especially where they eat.

A reptile hybrid with recessive avian genes. This particular farm animal is smart enough that it doesn’t need a pen and breeds faster than other reptiles. A very large appetite for a creature so small, which supports its ability to spit out nasty toxins on their enemies and create messes on the floor. Very easy to train and has a good memory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.label: chickenlizard
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlizard.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlizardEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized chickenlizard egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlizardEggFertilized.label: chickenlizard egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlizardEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized chickenlizard egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlizardEggUnfertilized.label: chickenlizard egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlodon.description: The chickenlodon is a very weird cross between one of the most fearsome predators ever existing... and a chicken. A saber-toothed chicken, if you will.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlodon.label: chickenlodon
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlodon.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlodon.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggFertilized.description: Fertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenlodon.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggFertilized.label: chickenlodon egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized chickenlodon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlodonEggUnfertilized.label: chickenlodon egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.description: The ideal companion for those who love the smell of fried eggs and napalm in the morning. Regretfully they’re far too birdbrained to receive any direction, and most owners have been noted saying “I’ve made a huge mistake”.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Very dangerous if allowed to breed uncontrollably, so consider liberal fireproofing and selling the excess. Produces chemfuel daily as well as an egg every 2 days.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.label: chickenlope
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenlope.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlopeEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickenlope egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlopeEggFertilized.label: chickenlope egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlopeEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized Chickenlope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenlopeEggUnfertilized.label: chickenlope egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickennathus.description: It is often said that birds are little more than evolved dinosaurs. Well, this small theropod hybrid is both a dinosaur AND a bird. Take that, science!\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Chickennathus are great egg layers.
LanguageData.GR_Chickennathus.label: chickennathus
LanguageData.GR_Chickennathus.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Chickennathus.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Chickennathus.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickennathus.
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggFertilized.label: chickennathus egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized chickennathus egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickennathusEggUnfertilized.label: chickennathus egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.description: One of the few unusual animal hybrids that come with their own warning labels despite being almost entirely harmless. All it took was one absent-minded vegan to cause a single couple of “Breeders” to overpopulate and devastate the ecology of an entire continent. Fairy tales surrounding the fluffy terror have done nothing to help it be taken seriously.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive rodent genes. An incredibly low metabolism coupled with an incredibly high reproduction rate, these animals are very sensitive to temperature and are unusually keen on escaping to the wilds.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.label: chickenrabbit
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenrabbit.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized Chickenrabbit egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggFertilized.label: chickenrabbit egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized Chickenrabbit egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The colonists swear it was coloured like this when the animal laid it.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenrabbitEggUnfertilized.label: chickenrabbit egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenspider.description:
For the unusual connoisseur who both enjoys a healthy breakfast and the feel of silk pyjamas. A very niche market, but considering the amount of money paid for the development, people like that are too rich to be considered mad. They’re simply eccentric with refined tastes, or “tossers” as the disenfranchised call them.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive avian genes. Quite large and difficult to train for an animal that lays eggs, this particular hybrid should always be kept warm due to the various functions taking up valuable space. Capable of producing not only eggs but also valuable silk, and is protected by thin, flexible scales to resist cuts.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenspider.label: chickenspider
LanguageData.GR_Chickenspider.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenspiderEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized Chickenspider egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. Since insectoid hybrids are always infertile, these eggs can never be fertilized.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenspiderEggUnfertilized.label: chickenspider egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.description: The Tiny Terror with a disconcertingly large mouth and sharp teeth. These comic flightless web-footed little bastards may finally unwittingly help man to fathom the uncharted depths of just how dangerous they can make a living creature the size of a basketball.\n\nAn avian hybrid with recessive canine genes. Doesn’t produce much in the way of eggs but makes up for it by having a shorter gestation period and a very nasty bite. They’re also incredibly smart and agile, making them ideal for swarm tactics.
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.label: chickenwolf
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Chickenwolf.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Chickenwolf egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggFertilized.label: chickenwolf egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized Chickenwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ChickenwolfEggUnfertilized.label: chickenwolf egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ChitinousHeart.description: A bionic heart implant using a reinforced shell of insectoid chitin for protection. This is a heart designed to take a beating. Pun intended, but it's the last one, I promise.
LanguageData.GR_ChitinousHeart.label: animal Implant: chitinous heart
LanguageData.GR_CommsTable.description: This was once a smart table. But now it's dumb.
LanguageData.GR_CommsTable.label: ancient comms table
LanguageData.GR_CompoundEye.description: This hybrid humanoid / insectoid eye provides improved vision in a wide range of different wavelengths, including those usually outside the human range, completely modifying the way the subject perceives their surroundings.
LanguageData.GR_CompoundEye.label: human Implant: compound eye
LanguageData.GR_CompressedChemfuel.description: A very volatile sludge with a tar-like appearance. Can be used defensively or converted to regular chemfuel in a refinery.
LanguageData.GR_CompressedChemfuel.label: deepchem
LanguageData.GR_CrocodileSpine.description: A bionic implant adding a sturdy crocodile spine to the creature's skeletal system.
LanguageData.GR_CrocodileSpine.label: animal Implant: crocodile spine
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.description:
A rather muscular hybrid with an impressively long snout, this ravenous reptile combines adaptability with mobility. Unusually, its terrifyingly long maw filled with curved teeth is not used to tear flesh to eat but to imbue toxins and the required muscles to spray those toxins out. This means they have to eat a lot more due to their subpar digestive system and their favourite activities include lounging in the sun and moving with disturbing quickness when the dinner bell is sounded. Also it… kinda looks like a weird shoe if you look at it, but, uh, don’t put your foot in there.

An equine hybrid with recessive reptile genes. A rather slow hybrid for its category, its main advantage is that it is very durable and it has a ranged toxic breath attack. Quite durable and comfortable in a wide range of temperatures, they present a hidden danger for those foolish enough to attack an otherwise easy target.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.label: crocorse
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Crocorse.verbs.0.label: poison breath
LanguageData.GR_CryoBlast.label: cryo blast
LanguageData.GR_DarkYoungWoodLog.description: This wood-like material has been extracted from the tentacles of an eldritch creature. Let's make a cupboard out of them!
LanguageData.GR_DarkYoungWoodLog.label: eldritch wood
LanguageData.GR_DevouringJaws.description: A set of improved teeth designed to rapidly chew and ingest any kind of food. Leaves kind of a mess, though...
LanguageData.GR_DevouringJaws.label: human Implant: devouring jaws
LanguageData.GR_DiggingBearClaws.description: Grotesque but powerful hand blades that have been modified to be used in mining operations, increasing both speed and yield.
LanguageData.GR_DiggingBearClaws.label: human Implant: digging mole claws
LanguageData.GR_DinoFeatherWool.description: This seems to be an amalgamation of feathers, scales and wool, perfectly interweaved. Gives adequate defense against blunt attacks, and is quite warm.
LanguageData.GR_DinoFeatherWool.label: dino featherwool
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenetic.description: A payload of dinosaur genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a dinosaur genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenetic.label: dinosaur genome
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_DinoGenomeExcavator.label: dino genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_DinoInsectJelly.description: Dinobeetles are huge beasts that produce a nutrient rich, amber-like substance. They probably would use it to build complex structures similar to bees, but they don't because... well, because they are a scientific abomination doomed from the start... Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.
LanguageData.GR_DinoInsectJelly.label: dino insect jelly
LanguageData.GR_DinoLeather.description: Thick leather partially covered in scales and dino-fuzz. Harvested from a dinosaur hybrid, this thick skin insulates and protects very well.
LanguageData.GR_DinoLeather.label: dinosaur leather
LanguageData.GR_DinoMilk.description: Triceramuffalos produce a secretion from various parts of their upper digestive tract. The resulting "milk" is fed to their offspring via regurgitation, and is similar to what mammals produce; it contains similar levels of fat and protein, along with carotenoids and antibodies. Now, why would anyone want to drink this? 'Cause it's the Rim, baby!
LanguageData.GR_DinoMilk.label: dino "milk"
LanguageData.GR_Diplobeetle.description: This huge sauropod hybrid has notable insectoid traits. It is hard to see how such a creature could have any predators, since it would probably leave its herbivorous diet and just eat them whole.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Diplobeetles produce a very nutritive jelly.
LanguageData.GR_Diplobeetle.label: diplobeetle
LanguageData.GR_Diplobeetle.tools.0.label: tail
LanguageData.GR_Diplobeetle.tools.1.label: left leg
LanguageData.GR_Diplobeetle.tools.2.label: right leg
LanguageData.GR_Disintegrator.label: disintegrator beam
LanguageData.GR_Distortion.label: spatial distortion
LanguageData.GR_DNAStorageBank.description:
A vital piece of archotechnology that is part of the process in creating an Archocentipede.

This machine is able to extract genetic material from live hybrids and stores it for later use, preventing deterioration. Depending on the base Genoframe, the amount of viable genetic material extracted will vary.

Each storage bank can only store the genetic material from a single animal branch, such as ursine or feline. Inserting a hybrid into the database yields half as much valuable data compared to a paragon. DNA storage banks need to be in line of sight of the archocentipede former to connect to it properly.

Detailed yields for paragons (half for hybrids):

Awful quality: yields 1%
Poor quality: yields 3%
Normal quality: yields 6%
Good quality: yields 12%
Excellent quality: yields 25%
Masterwork quality: yields 50%
Legendary quality: yields 100%
LanguageData.GR_DNAStorageBank.label: DNA storage bank
LanguageData.GR_Doedicoon.description: This armoured rodent is an hybrid of a raccoon and a Doedicurus. The Doedicoon breeds fast, and its armor can be useful in the frontlines.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher. Armoured rodent
LanguageData.GR_Doedicoon.label: doedicoon
LanguageData.GR_Doedicoon.tools.0.label: tail
LanguageData.GR_Doedicoon.tools.1.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Doedicoon.tools.2.label: right claw
