LanguageData.GR_Dogman.description:
Man’s best friend who is also himself? Have you ever thought that the folds on a dog’s skin can act almost as pockets? What if those folds held a knife and the dog could wield it? Well look no further, as this pup will answer those questions with violence.

A canine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A smart hybrid capable of holding conversations with their technical superiors and will sometimes obey commands to slaughter other tamed colony animals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Dogmen will periodically obey commands to slaughter other colony (tamed) animals.
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.label: dogman
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Dogman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_EggBomb.description: This huge egg is filled with unstable chemicals. It is going to explode!
LanguageData.GR_EggBomb.label: detonating unstable egg
LanguageData.GR_EggMeadowLizardFertilized.description: Fertilized meadow lizard egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby meadow lizard. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_EggMeadowLizardFertilized.label: meadow lizard egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EggNighthrumboFertilized.description: Fertilized nighthrumbo egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a baby nighthrumbo. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_EggNighthrumboFertilized.label: nighthrumbo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_Elasmobearium.description: The Elasmobearium is a hybrid of a bear and the ancient ancestor of the rhinoceros. The Elasmobearium is a pretty aggressive omnivore, using its horn to impale its victims and then mauling them with their vicious paws.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary bear hybrids have stun attacks. Aside from obviously being a very powerful creature, they produce a wool of excellent quality.
LanguageData.GR_Elasmobearium.label: elasmobearium
LanguageData.GR_Elasmobearium.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Elasmobearium.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Elasmobearium.tools.3.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBear.description: This otherworldly bear has repulsive, purple coloured tentacles protruding from its maw. Its pelt has a strange wooden-like appearance. Its ravenous appetite can only be sated by consuming large amounts of meat, which the creature dissolves with the green acidic goo dropping from its many gaping maws.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bear hybrids have stun attacks. Since the creature constantly regenerates, its tentacles can be trimmed every few days, and used as a substitute for wood.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBear.label: bear young
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBear.tools.0.label: hooves
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBear.tools.1.label: tentacles
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBoomalope.description: This polymorphic entity is made of light brown ichor and looks like amorphous goo. It seems to be extremely resilient and very difficult to dispatch. Its pelt seems to be powerfully acidic in substance and can dissolve human flesh with even a slight touch.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Explodes on death with a very powerful psionic blast. This explosion can also be triggered on command, though it will damage the creature too!
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBoomalope.label: formless boomalope of Tsathoggua
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBoomalope.tools.0.label: amoebic tentacle
LanguageData.GR_EldritchBoomalope.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChicken.description: An unholy amalgamation of fungus, crustacean, insect and... chicken. Can this creature even be considered to be truly alive?\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This psionic chicken lays A LOT of purple eggs that erode your sanity. What's not to love?
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChicken.label: chicken of Mi-go
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChicken.tools.0.label: overwhelming mind
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChicken.tools.1.label: body
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggFertilized.descript: Fertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chicken of Mi-go.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggFertilized.label: chicken of Mi-go egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggUnfertilized.descri: Unfertilized chicken of Mi-go egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchChickenEggUnfertilized.label: chicken of Mi-go egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_EldritchExcavator.label: eldritch genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_EldritchGenetic.description: A payload of eldritch genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via an eldritch genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_EldritchGenetic.label: eldritch genome
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectJelly.description: Jelly stored and used as food by oversized insects. It is smooth, rich, and brings incredible joy to those who consume it. Also, it quickly erodes their sanity and turns them into insane psychopaths, but it's so tasty! Because of its unique biological properties, it can nourish almost any creature and never rots.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectJelly.label: eldritch insect jelly
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectoid.description: This engorged, tick-like creature has a writhing mass of tentacles where its head would normally be, sensing their surroundings by a combination of touch and echolocation. They have lost their capacity to burrow underground, but they are capable of powerful psionic attacks.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Produces eldritch insect jelly, a very nutritive and enjoyable food that quickly erodes your sanity.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectoid.label: armoured chthonian larva
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectoid.tools.0.label: grasping tentacles
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectoid.tools.1.label: overwhelming mind
LanguageData.GR_EldritchInsectoid.tools.2.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMilk.description: This milk is purple coloured, and its smell is indescribable. It whispers to you...
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMilk.label: eldritch milk
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMuffalo.description: This blind muffalo can detect its prey by scent, and is attracted to animal -and human- blood, although it can also consume vegetal matter.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Decent pack beast that doesn't need to be penned. Produces milk and wool. Purple milk and wool...
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMuffalo.label: star vampire muffalo
LanguageData.GR_EldritchMuffalo.tools.0.label: vampiric teeth
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptile.description: This multi-headed snake has been blessed by Yig, the Father of Serpents. Or maybe it is just a very unfortunate snake which has been the target of your colonists' mad science... It is most likely the latter, to be honest.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. The snake of Yig has six mouths, and attacks incredibly fast.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptile.label: snake of Yig
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptile.tools.0.label: toxic bite
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggFertilized.descript: Fertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting. If all goes well, it should hatch into an snake of Yig egg.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggFertilized.label: snake of Yig egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggUnfertilized.descri: Unfertilized snake of Yig egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. The purple shell is incredibly disconcerting.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchReptileEggUnfertilized.label: snake of Yig egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_EldritchThrumbo.description: IÃ¤! IÃ¤! Cthrumbo fhtagn! Ph'nglui mglw'nfah Cthrumbo Rimworld wgah'nagl fhtagn! A gigantic, squid-like creature that feeds on fear and nightmares. Legends say that an Eldritch Cthrumbo is really the wisest creature in the universe - it simply would melt the minds of everyone near it should it choose to speak.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Invoke insanity ability: twice a day, the Cthrumbo can release a cloud of insanity that erodes its prey's mind.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchThrumbo.label: cthrumbo
LanguageData.GR_EldritchThrumbo.tools.0.label: rending claws
LanguageData.GR_EldritchThrumbo.tools.1.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWolf.description: The back of this feral looking wolf is an indistinct mass of tentacles and malign eyes, constantly shifting in place and being reabsorbed into its pelt again.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. This creature doesn't have a huge health pool, but it is nearly impervious to damage.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWolf.label: wolf-Sothoth
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWolf.tools.0.label: venomous fangs
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWool.description: This purple wool seems to be irrigated by pulsing, throbbing purple veins. However, they can only be detected by sight, not by touch.
LanguageData.GR_EldritchWool.label: eldritch wool
LanguageData.GR_ElectroEgg.description: This diminutive egg seems to be metallic in nature at first, yet it is soft enough to crush it between two fingers. Once thus crushed it can be swallowed normally, and the active mechanites inside have a reinvigorating effect on the consumer. This effect can be slightly addictive, though.
LanguageData.GR_ElectroEgg.label: mechanoid egg
LanguageData.GR_ElectroWomb.description: A relatively small machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides limited growing room for specimens.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> This electrowomb is very cheap to build, but will produce a failure if the inserted creature has a body size bigger than 1.
LanguageData.GR_ElectroWomb.label: electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_EquineGenetic.description:
A payload of equine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids have increased caravaning speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_EquineGenetic.label: equine genome
LanguageData.GR_FeatherdustJoint.description: Chickenbear feathers can be gathered and processed into a fine powder, which produces a mild euphoric sensation when burned and inhaled. Featherdust use can produce a dependency.
LanguageData.GR_FeatherdustJoint.label: featherdust joint
LanguageData.GR_FelineGenetic.description:
A payload of feline genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All feline hybrids have an inherent dodge chance. Combat feline hybrids get a further increase to dodge chance.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_FelineGenetic.label: feline genome
LanguageData.GR_Filth_Fuel.label: chemfuel puddle
LanguageData.GR_FilthMucus.label: mucus
LanguageData.GR_FireSpit.label: fire spit
LanguageData.GR_FleshFlies.description: A swarm of small, deadly flies. Almost impossible to hit using rudimentary weapons, the ideal way of dealing with fleshflies is by using fire, to which they are highly susceptible.
LanguageData.GR_FleshFlies.label: flesh flies
LanguageData.GR_FleshFlies.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_FleshFliesGas.label: flesh flies
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth.description: Something went terribly wrong. A huge and weird growth has spawned within the electrowomb, breaking it completely and spreading all around. This growth is a hatchery for small flies that can devour any living creature to the bone. It seems to be sensitive to daylight, but no one knows how far it can spread at night.
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth.label: flesh growth
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth_Building.description: Something went terribly wrong. A huge and weird growth has spawned within the electrowomb, breaking it completely and spreading all around. This growth is a hatchery for small flies that can devour any living creature to the bone. It seems to be sensitive to daylight, but no one knows how far it can spread at night.
LanguageData.GR_FleshGrowth_Building.label: flesh growth
LanguageData.GR_Fleshling.description: Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. Whilst it doesn’t pose any real threat and it seems quite friendly, it requires a tremendous amount of love and attention for it to find the strength to live another day.
LanguageData.GR_Fleshling.label: fleshling
LanguageData.GR_Fleshling.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_FleshMonstrosity.description: Something went terribly wrong. This wretched creature is struggling to keep breathing. It’s rabid, and the moment it crawled out of the vat it started to look for the next unfortunate living being to destroy. It can and should be slain as soon as possible, as each additional kill only prolongs its life.
LanguageData.GR_FleshMonstrosity.label: flesh monstrosity
LanguageData.GR_FleshMonstrosity.tools.0.label: bite
LanguageData.GR_FossilChemfuel.description: Deprecated. No hybrid should produce this now.
LanguageData.GR_FossilChemfuel.label: fossil chemfuel
LanguageData.GR_Gas_Dust.label: dust
LanguageData.GR_Gas_Ice.label: ice crystals
LanguageData.GR_GenePod.description:
A machine where the miracle - or horror - of birth happens using automated procedures, a little genetic hand-waving and a great heaping dose of lost sleep. The inserted Genoframe is influenced by two genomes, one dominant and one recessive, and can be modified with optional boosters.

The process isn’t perfect, due to material contamination, missing chromosomes or simple bad luck, and can result in something completely unwanted being developed. For the machine, it makes no difference whether it’s trying to grow an animal or a comically large fruit. The resulting growth cell has a limited lifespan as the embryonic fluid will start breaking down if not allowed to gestate properly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> If the process to deliver ingredients to the genomorpher is interrupted, the ingredients that have already been inserted in it will deteriorate and be lost.
LanguageData.GR_GenePod.label: genomorpher
LanguageData.GR_GeneRecombinator.description: A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into an extremely complex genome. Completely useless by itself, but functions as a template which can be compared to another genome. The procedure removes any excess genetic information and renders them completely identical.\n\nBear in mind that this machine cannot duplicate some genomes, such as colossal.
LanguageData.GR_GeneRecombinator.label: genome recombinator
LanguageData.GR_GeneticExtractionTable.description: A specialized extraction module that is equipped to extract the genomes from a subject’s corpse. The material is converted into a soupish-mixture which is then condensed into the relevant genome. Processing speed is influenced by intelligence, as it relies on knowing which button to press, when to press it and where to find it under the intimidating number of submenus.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The genome extractor table will only work for tier 1 genomes. For tier 2 (insectoids, reptiles, equines) and tier 3 (colossals) you will need genome excavators, built on the genetics tinkering table or found on abandoned labs.
LanguageData.GR_GeneticExtractionTable.label: genome extractor table
LanguageData.GR_GeneticsTinkeringTable.description: An advanced and specialized workbench capable of manufacturing medical-grade implants and mixing genetic boosters specifically tuned to Genomorphers. These include but aren’t limited to boosters that improve the safety or stability of hybrids and even micro-controllers that enable direct user-input.
LanguageData.GR_GeneticsTinkeringTable.label: genetics tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeAwful.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nAwful genoframe will result in a hybrid with greatly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeAwful.label: Genoframe (Awful)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeExcellent.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nExcellent genoframe will result in a hybrid with significantly improved stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeExcellent.label: Genoframe (Excellent)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeGood.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nGood genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly improved stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeGood.label: Genoframe (Good)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeLegendary.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nLegendary genoframe will result in an incredibly powerful hybrid thatâ€™s eclipsing normal creatures.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeLegendary.label: Genoframe (Legendary)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeMasterwork.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nMasterwork genoframe will result in a hybrid with superior stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeMasterwork.label: Genoframe (Masterwork)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeNormal.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nNormal genoframe will result in a hybrid with baseline stats.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeNormal.label: Genoframe (Normal)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframePoor.description: A stable genetic material consumed by the genomorpher machine that serves as a framework for hybrid experimentation. After the framework is used to create a growthcell, the genoframe is empty and discarded.\n\nPoor genoframe will result in a hybrid with slightly decreased stats over a normal hybrid.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframePoor.label: Genoframe (Poor)
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeUnfinished.description: An unfinished genoframe of some kind.
LanguageData.GR_GenoframeUnfinished.label: unfinished genoframe
LanguageData.GR_GenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_GenomeExcavator.label: genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_GreatHairballProjectile.label: greater hairball
LanguageData.GR_GreenMilk.description: This Lizardochs milk is green coloured, and quite unappetizing.
LanguageData.GR_GreenMilk.label: green milk
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.description: The horned groundffalo is a great pack beast (albeit a bit slow) capable of producing milk and wool, as well as mining for your colony. Compared to a Groundrunner it won't be as efficient on its mining work, but the other characteristics make up for this.\n\nUseful abilities: Produces wool, milk, acts as a pack beast and mines rocks and minerals.
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.label: groundffalo
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.2.label: horns
LanguageData.GR_Groundffalo.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_GrowthCell.description: A portable, human-made casing designed to hold an embryo until it is transferred to a relevant Electrowomb, similar to fertilized eggs that require incubation. The embryonic fluid is unstable due to the unnatural genetic combination, so it will need to be implanted within 7 days after its creation. This growth cell has to be kept at a temperature between 0 and 50 degrees Celsius.
LanguageData.GR_GrowthCell.label: growth cell
LanguageData.GR_HairballProjectile.label: hairball
LanguageData.GR_Hairballs.label: hairballs
LanguageData.GR_HibernationModule.description: A biochip that modifies the brain to reduce the need for sleep.
LanguageData.GR_HibernationModule.label: human Implant: hibernation module
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatPelt.label: bearcat pelt
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatRug.description: A bearcat pelt rug. This thing seems to be quite more exotic than other pelt rugs!
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearCatRug.label: huge bearcat rug
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearPelt.label: huge bear pelt
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearRug.description: A bear pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_HugeBearRug.label: huge bear rug
LanguageData.GR_HumanoidGenetic.description:
A payload of humanoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All humanoid hybrids have a somewhat decent level of intelligence and can do various tasks around the colony.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from any sentient humanoid.
LanguageData.GR_HumanoidGenetic.label: humanoid genome
LanguageData.GR_HumanThrumboHorn.description: A genetically modified thrumbo horn that can be implanted in a subject's forehead. Nasty.
LanguageData.GR_HumanThrumboHorn.label: human Implant: thrumbo horn
LanguageData.GR_Hurseman.description:
An extremely cursed riding companion that can vocally explain in excruciating detail the troubles it goes through as a beast of burden. Most people don’t like to be reminded that animals are capable of feeling, especially if they can vocalise themselves in human speech. Don’t be surprised if no one wants to eat or wear butchered parts of this creature, as it looks like a misshapen human with cloven hands.

An equine hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very intelligent and relatively low maintenance, they make for a decent rideable pack animal. Their main advantage comes in their ability to cheer up the travellers in a caravan, however it doesn’t understand social cues and can throw out some devastating social commentary.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). The hurseman engages caravan members too while caravaning, causing mood boosts (up to +15). 10% of the time however it will insult them, making them loose mood (up to -30). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
LanguageData.GR_Hurseman.label: hurseman
LanguageData.GR_Hurseman.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Hurseman.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Hurseman.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_HybridChitin.description: Overlapping plates of hardened chitin. Really durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
LanguageData.GR_HybridChitin.label: hybrid chitin
LanguageData.GR_HybridScales.description: Light, pliable sheets of scaly skin. Light, strong, and insulates well.
LanguageData.GR_HybridScales.label: hybrid scales
LanguageData.GR_HybridSilk.description: Fabric spun from silk fibers. Resists blunt and sharp damage easily, but provides almost no protection against extreme heat.
LanguageData.GR_HybridSilk.label: hybrid silk
LanguageData.GR_IguanaScales.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to high temperatures.
LanguageData.GR_IguanaScales.label: human Implant: iguana scales
LanguageData.GR_ImprovedController.description: Self-assembling machinery that fuses with the specimen’s nerve centers during the growth process. Effectively allows hybrids to be drafted.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_ImprovedController.label: improved controller
LanguageData.GR_IncendiaryMote.label: incendiary bolt
LanguageData.GR_Insanity_Cloud.label: insanity cloud
LanguageData.GR_InsectCloudMotes.label: insect clouds
LanguageData.GR_InsectileEye.description: A bionic implant which gives the creature compound insectile eyes. Compared with simple eyes, compound eyes possess a very large view angle, and can detect fast movement.
LanguageData.GR_InsectileEye.label: animal Implant: insectile eye
LanguageData.GR_InsectMandibles.description: A bionic implant adding insect mandibles to the creature's face. Insect mandibles improve manipulation and eating, while also working as an offensive weapon
LanguageData.GR_InsectMandibles.label: animal Implant: insect mandibles
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidAntennae.description: These hybrid humanoid / insectoid feeling appendages can be implanted in a subject's head, completely modifying the way the subject "sees" and feels the surrounding world. The experience may be slightly overwhelming due to sensorial overload.
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidAntennae.label: human Implant: insectoid antennae
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidGenetic.description:
A payload of insectoid genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All hybrids with insectoid genome get 100% toxic resistance as well as an armor increase. Combat hybrids get a further increase to armor.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidGenetic.label: insectoid genome
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidPheromones.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with insectoid genes to give the ability to detect pheromones, thus improving the olfactory system. Can't be installed on birds and (ironically) insects.
LanguageData.GR_InsectoidPheromones.label: animal Implant: pheromone receivers
LanguageData.GR_KidneyToxicFilters.description: This is a bioelectronic kidney prosthesis that greatly improves the efficiency of the subject's kidney by using insectoid tissue, providing resistance to toxic substances.
LanguageData.GR_KidneyToxicFilters.label: human Implant: kidney toxic filters
LanguageData.GR_LabDoor.description: Divides rooms. Simple doors must be manually opened, which slows people down. The amount of slowdown depends on what the door is made of.
LanguageData.GR_LabDoor.label: lab door
LanguageData.GR_LabLockerBank.description: An ancient, rusted bank of lockers. All of the lockers have been forced open with nothing valuable left behind.
LanguageData.GR_LabLockerBank.label: ancient lab locker bank
LanguageData.GR_LargeElectroWomb.description: A larger machine designed to simulate the environment of a mother’s womb. Once a Growthcell has been inserted, the embryo can start growing all the way to its adult life stage before being released. Provides more growing room for larger specimens.
LanguageData.GR_LargeElectroWomb.label: large electrowomb
LanguageData.GR_Laser.label: laser beam
LanguageData.GR_LizardLeg.description: A bionic animal leg reinforced with tough lizard skin.
LanguageData.GR_LizardLeg.label: animal Implant: lizard leg
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.description:
It is doubtful that one would meet a crueller or more sadistic creature than this hybrid. It actively experiences joy when slaughtering tame animals and executing prisoners, the latter of which even provides a powerful boost which can be used before battle. Maybe it has something to do with taking pleasure in killing those that made it this way, but that’s a story for another time.

A reptile hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. A very intelligent but combat-limited animal that performs surprisingly poorly against similar sized creatures. They will only passively engage creatures much smaller than itself and takes some twisted, psychotic pleasure in killing those who are completely helpless.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. Lizardmen will obey order to slaughter other colony animals, as well as execute prisoners of the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.label: lizardman
LanguageData.GR_Lizardman.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.description: The lizardochs is basically a reptilian bull, an hybrid between a lizard and the ancestor of domesticated cattle. The lizardochs needs little substenance to survive, at least for a creature of its size, and can be used as a pack animal, which makes it ideal to use in caravans through warm and dry lands such as deserts.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks. Produces milk, and acts as a pack animal that needs little food.
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.label: lizardochs
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.tools.2.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Lizardochs.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_LooseCables.description: This tangle of decayed cables were probably once housed inside the ceiling panels.
LanguageData.GR_LooseCables.label: loose cables
LanguageData.GR_LuxuriantHybridWool.description: This wool is extremely thick and warm. Probably the best wool in the animal kingdom.
LanguageData.GR_LuxuriantHybridWool.label: luxuriant hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_Manalope.description:
A floating, ticking time bomb that produces extremely reactive chemicals instead of chemfuel. Arguably more of a complex chemical distillery than an animal, it's a scientific miracle that it doesn’t explode every time it coughs. Surprisingly effective in combat in ways the enemy would really have preferred not knowing.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Thanks to its sac filled with buoyant chemicals, this hybrid never touches the ground and simply floats from location to location. Ignores terrain penalty costs and traps, while also being able to set down explosive traps of its own.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Set trap ability: once every 3 days the manalope can set up an explosive IED. Hovers over terrain, ignoring terrain costs, and doesn't trigger ANY traps.
LanguageData.GR_Manalope.label: manalope
LanguageData.GR_Manalope.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Manbear.description:
What it lacks in ferocity, it makes up for in tenacity and formidable cuteness. What person, sane or insane, could reject the diplomatic arguments presented by a waist-high teddy bear with a face just begging to be pinched. Not that you actually would, as those sharp teeth remind one that it very likely prefers to debate with its food before eating it.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Absolutely terrible in combat potential despite its genetic material, but it is surprisingly adept at convincing prisoners to join the colony.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Menbear are adept conversationalists and will help in recruiting prisoners.
LanguageData.GR_Manbear.label: manbear
LanguageData.GR_Manbear.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.GR_Manbear.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.GR_Manbear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mancat.description:
A creature originally designed for other recreational purposes before that particular laboratory of degenerates got glassed from orbit, they make for an affectionate conversation partner. You know, probably, most likely, until their particular speech pattern grinds on your nerves like a cheese grater.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive feline genes. A surprisingly large yet timid predator that can output a disturbing amount of damage. Typically fun to talk to, but 10% of the time it tries to be a little too cute.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). In the case of the mancat, these interactions will 90% of the time increase the targets' social joy by 15%, but the rest of the time they'll just make the target very annoyed... If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
LanguageData.GR_Mancat.label: mancat
LanguageData.GR_Mancat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Manchicken.description:
“You know how, like, chicken tastes, like, so good? So what if, like, we made a chicken that, y’know, when you killed it you could, I dunno, stuff it right in your mouth as fried chicken? Dude, this shit is the shit, where did you get this, man?” - Recording playback used in a hearing against a disgraced genetic scientist for misappropriating government funding.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive avian genes. A very valuable hybrid that convinces people that its greatest desire in life is to be eaten. When butchered, it leaves large amounts of completely healthy and nutritious meat that can be eaten as-is.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Doesn't lay eggs, but can be butchered for large amounts of a very nutritive meat that doesn't have poisoning chance and takes 6 days to rot.
LanguageData.GR_Manchicken.label: manchicken
LanguageData.GR_Manchicken.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Manchicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Manffalo.description:
Considered a straight upgrade from their progenitor, featured several times in Cannibal Monthly. Produces appreciable amounts of wool and milk, it is a living, breathing reference to the phrase “what does not kill me makes me stronger”. At least, when it comes to hauling large loads, which is all some people care about.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Capable of surviving very high temperatures in opposition to the traditional use of muffalos, this hybrid performs below average for a pack animal. However, due to adaptive muscle and skeletal structure, the more it participates as part of a caravan, the heavier the load it can withstand.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. The manffalo starts as a poor caravaning animal, but learns as time progresses. Every hour spent in a caravan will increase its cargo capacity by 1%, up to a maximum of a 100% increase from base.
LanguageData.GR_Manffalo.label: manffalo
LanguageData.GR_Manffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Manscarab.description:
They say you can learn a lot by taking something apart and putting it back together again. Not this creature, it doesn’t give a damn, it just wants to take things apart because the hardest part is someone else’s problem. Some people actually prefer these chitinous creatures over actual humans as they’re not going to use up good material in a botched attempt.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Easy to train, quite resilient but not a great combatant. This particular hybrid fills the niche of fulfilling deconstruction orders around the colony, freeing up otherwise valuable manpower.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Menscarab will take building deconstruction orders around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Manscarab.label: manscarab
LanguageData.GR_Manscarab.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_Manscarab.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mansquirrel.description:
A bloody weird little thing that hates the cold with a passion, and will take any opportunity to clear away snow from its home. Given that they are active at nighttime, they make a great conversational companion for those otherwise asocial Night Owls you keep gathering.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite quick for their size and unusually a nocturnal animal, they make for a fast, social and quick-breeding hybrid. Will shovel snow if there are Clear Snow zones set up.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. The mansquirrel is a nocturnal animal that sleeps during the day. Will obey orders to clear snow around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Mansquirrel.label: mansquirrel
LanguageData.GR_Mansquirrel.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Mansquirrel.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Mansquirrel.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mantistanis.description: Mixing the genes of one of the biggest carnivorous birds ever existing and those of a praying mantis is either a genious idea, or anathema. The Mantistanis stalks its prey by standing incredibly still and launching itself to attack with lightning speed.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Slight regenerative properties.
LanguageData.GR_Mantistanis.label: mantistanis
LanguageData.GR_Mantistanis.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Mantistanis.tools.1.label: mantis blades
LanguageData.GR_Mantistanis.tools.2.label: mantis blades
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggFertilized.description: Fertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a mantistanis.
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggFertilized.label: mantistanis egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized mantistanis egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_MantistanisEggUnfertilized.label: mantistanis egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Manwolf.description:
Possibly either the ancestor to the warg or an attempt at creating a successor, the team in charge of making this particular hybrid clearly had read one to many mythological novels. Arguably there’s no transformation at the full moon, so you just get the permanent single form, but there’s no pleasing some people.

A humanoid hybrid with recessive canine genes. A fast and dangerous hunter with almost misplaced self-confidence. Also very intelligent and possessing faint regenerative capabilities. Extremely hardy but also extremely restrictive in their diet.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts.
LanguageData.GR_Manwolf.label: manwolf
LanguageData.GR_Manwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Manwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Manwolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.description: The meadow lizard is a beast bred for speed. Even though they are naturally slower than meadow aves, they can be trained to be incredibly fast mounts.\n\nUseful abilities: if properly trained they can be ridden into battle at incredible speeds (this interaction needs the Giddy-Up, combat mounts mod, and it will make the Meadow Lizards move 100% faster when used as mounts). They are also venomous.
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.label: meadow lizard
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_MeadowLizard.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechabear.description: This cybernetic beast has been mutated far beyond its original form. Heavily armoured to withstand heavy fire, and armed with a terrifying plasma cannon.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechabear an effective weapons platform, akin to a rocket turret. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechabear.label: mechabear
LanguageData.GR_Mechabear.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechabear.verbs.0.label: plasma burst
LanguageData.GR_Mechacat.description: The tail of this robotic creature has been crafted from an amalgamation of scyther blade structures and mechanite self-replicators. This allows the mechacat to use its tail as a throwable weapon, and quickly regenerate it to use it again. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechacat capable of attacking with slashing attacks from a range. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechacat.label: mechacat
LanguageData.GR_Mechacat.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechacat.verbs.0.label: razor tail
LanguageData.GR_Mechachicken.description: This cybernetic organism is adept at reconnaissance and infiltration, due to its small size and speed. It is armed with a small sniper flechette gun. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-chicken an effective weapons platform with very long range sniper attacks. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechachicken.label: mecha-chicken
LanguageData.GR_Mechachicken.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechachicken.verbs.0.label: sniper flechette
LanguageData.GR_Mechafuse.description:
This piece of technology is unusual in that its design isn’t linked to the original Mechahybridizer, having been developed later on while trying to emulate the system internals. This is due to the fact that the Mechahybridizer broadcasts a signal to a nearby orbital mechanoid hive to protect the process and then recover the potential resulting mechahybrid to be dispatched into assignments. However, this process is extremely expensive and often there would be enough recovery personnel on-site, so this device was developed to reduce costs.

In the event that the Mechahybridizer attempts to send a signal once it begins the operation, this device intercepts the signal before it can extend to a sufficient distance. Upon capture, the device self-destructs to fool the system that the signal has been received and a unit has been dispatched.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.
LanguageData.GR_Mechafuse.label: mechafuse
LanguageData.GR_MechaHeart.description: A bionic heart strengthened by the action of active mechanites.
LanguageData.GR_MechaHeart.label: animal Implant: reinforced bionic heart
LanguageData.GR_Mechahorse.description: More machine than horse, the mecha-horse has an agile frame covered by ablative panels. Though well-liked by riders for its speed and agility, it has a significant design flaw: it heats up considerably during extended travels, needing to periodically vent excess heated air generated on their internal machinery.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-horse capable of using its "vent heated exhaust" ability to scorch enemies on the surrounding tiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechahorse.label: mecha-horse
LanguageData.GR_Mechahorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechahorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Mechahorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_MechahybridAntenna.description: A large broadcasting server that is specifically tuned to maintain the tameness of mechahybrids. Without this server, the self-replicating mechanites within the mechahybrids will eventually no longer be able to register friendlies as their IFF updates and branches, causing them to go manhunter.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> It is important to note that a single antenna can only broadcast to a limited number of mechahybrids, specifically up to 5 of them regardless of their size or complexity. Any additionally mechahybrid present beyond the initial 5 will no longer receive updates and will eventually lose their "tameness". Part of the broadcast will reach nearby settlements and ruins, attracting maddened mechahybrids, so they will appear from the moment you have a mechahybrid antenna in the periodic roaming monstrosities raids.
LanguageData.GR_MechahybridAntenna.label: mechahybrid antenna
LanguageData.GR_Mechahybridizer.description:
A massive factory designed to improve upon genetically perfect animals by adding doses of mechanites in a controlled fashion and influencing the process to ensure the it is a success and there is no rejection of foreign materials. Once the operation begins on a creature designated as "paragon", the factory closes with hermetic seals and broadcasts a recovery signal to the nearest orbital mechanoid hive.

Originally designed to be part of an automated mechanoid factory placed on animal worlds, the intent was to herd animals into the system, weaponize them, and send them off for their deployment. Due to the invasive nature of the mechanites, the creature absolutely had to lack junk genetic data lest the subject perish and waste materials. Later on, the lack of such paragons resulted in it being installed in facilities with genetic modification equipment on hand and external bypasses to reduce the waste material produced from summoning a retrieval team.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> If there are no intact fuses around a mechahybridizer, a mechanoid raid will arrive via drop-pod in 6 hours if the mechahybridization process is attempted.
LanguageData.GR_Mechahybridizer.label: mechahybridizer
LanguageData.GR_Mechalope.description: This cybernetic boomalope has been outfitted with four mechanized chemfuel reservoirs. The creature can use these reservoirs to produce a jet of burning fuel similar to a flamethrower, not incredibly harmful, but perfect for starting huge fires.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechalope an effective weapons platform, akin to a flamethrower. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechalope.label: mechalope
LanguageData.GR_Mechalope.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechalope.verbs.0.label: chemfuel flamethrower
LanguageData.GR_MechaMilk.description: This metallic, quicksilver-like substance is not edible. It can be processed in a refinery to obtain neutroamine, chemfuel and a bit of silver.
LanguageData.GR_MechaMilk.label: mechanoid milk
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.description:
The ultimate violation of all ethical concerns in genetic engineering: a humanlike mechanoid creature with obvious malevolence. Created by warping humanoid genes into a dangerous Mime, and then further modifying it with self-replicating mechanites by using forgotten technology, the mecha-mime will need to be kept in a short leash, lest its IFF system goes haywire and decides to enact its very own judgement day.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster. The mecha-mime has lost the infiltration abilities of a regular mime, but in exchange it possesses a powerful spatial warping cannon that ignores enemy's armor. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.

<color=#E5E54C>Warning:</color> A mecha-mime's IFF sensors will fail in only 2 days, instead of 5.
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.label: mecha-mime
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechamime.verbs.0.label: spatial distortion
LanguageData.GR_Mechamuffalo.description: This hulking cybernetic organism has been reinforced with an adaptive plasteel mesh, enabling it to carry an incredible amount of stuff. The mecha-muffalo is the perfect pack animal, but it is quite useless in an offensive role. Defensively, however, it is armed with a smoke cannon that it can use to cover the battlefield in a dense cloud of dust.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mecha-muffalo capable of laying a screen of smoke for defensive purposes. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechamuffalo.label: mecha-muffalo
LanguageData.GR_Mechamuffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechamuffalo.verbs.0.label: smoke cannon
LanguageData.GR_MechaniteBooster.description: This highly sought-after booster will result in an almost perfect hybrid: fertile, draftable and genetically stable.\n\nThis booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.
LanguageData.GR_MechaniteBooster.label: mechanite booster
LanguageData.GR_MechanoidChip.description: This strange stellated octahedron seems to house an assembly of complex computer chips. It appears to be self-powered, and a faint beeping noise emanates from its core. It is constantly emitting encoded radio waves, and perhaps these could be suitably amplified if the technology behind it could be properly reverse engineered.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid chips can only be found in abandoned labs, and can be used to build beomechanical lab beacons, that will allow you to find a biomechanical lab complex, as well as mechahybrid antennas.
LanguageData.GR_MechanoidChip.label: mechanoid chip
LanguageData.GR_Mecharat.description: This tiny cybernetic organism can be mass-produced as a kind of "suicide weapon". It is very fast, and armed with a short ranged stun gun, so hordes of mecha-rats can be sent to the front lines to keep enemies stunned and in check. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, allowing the colonists to send the mecha-rat to the front lines to quickly stun enemies. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mecharat.label: mecha-rat
LanguageData.GR_Mecharat.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mecharat.verbs.0.label: stun bolt
LanguageData.GR_MechaReceptors.description: This elephant auditory receptor can scan a really wide band of frequencies.
LanguageData.GR_MechaReceptors.label: animal Implant: elephant auditory receptor
LanguageData.GR_Mechaspider.description: This cybernetic organism has traits from both its insectoid and mechanoid origins. The mechaspider is armed with a powerful twin-coiled laser machine gun, perfect to obliterate enemies in a rain of laser fire.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechaspider an effective weapons platform with a very high fire rate. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechaspider.label: mechaspider
LanguageData.GR_Mechaspider.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechaspider.verbs.0.label: twin-coiled laser
LanguageData.GR_MechaSpine.description: A bionic implant adding a plasteel structure with active mechanites to the creature's skeletal system.
LanguageData.GR_MechaSpine.label: animal Implant: reinforced bionic spine
LanguageData.GR_MechaTail.description: An advanced animal tail, even more powerful than a natural one. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_MechaTail.label: animal Implant: reinforced tail
LanguageData.GR_Mechathrumbo.description: This hulking cybernetic behemoth is why you don't need tanks in the Rim. Its huge plasteel carapace hides a miniaturized synchrotron that constantly generates small amounts of antimatter, which is instantly used to fill a miniature antigrain warhead. Slow, but quite effective.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechathrumbo effective mobile artillery. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechathrumbo.label: mechathrumbo
LanguageData.GR_Mechathrumbo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechathrumbo.verbs.0.label: antigrain artillery
LanguageData.GR_Mechaturtle.description: This slow, cybernetic beast is heavily armoured with a plasteel-encased biomechanical shell. It is armed with a poison spitting cannon.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechaturtle an effective weapons platform that fires venomous projectiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechaturtle.label: mechaturtle
LanguageData.GR_Mechaturtle.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechaturtle.verbs.0.label: poison blast
LanguageData.GR_Mechawolf.description: This cybernetic beast has been augmented with a powerful synthetic structure capable of generating waves of cryogenic liquid. \n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color>Mechanoid hybrids are always created with a controller booster, making the mechawolf an effective weapons platform that fires frost projectiles. Mechanoid hybrids need very little food or rest, don't have psychic or toxic sensitivity, and have devastating ranged attacks. They have however high wildness, making them harder to keep tamed, and they will need a mechahybrid antenna to be built somewhere on the planet, or their IFF sensors will malfunction in 5 days. One antenna is needed for each five mech hybrids under the colony's control.
LanguageData.GR_Mechawolf.label: mechawolf
LanguageData.GR_Mechawolf.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Mechawolf.verbs.0.label: cryo cannon
LanguageData.GR_MechaWool.description: This wool seems to be strengthened by plasteel micro-fibers, and covered with advanced circuitry.
LanguageData.GR_MechaWool.label: mechanoid wool
LanguageData.GR_MechLabWall.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_MechLabWall.label: mech laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenetic.description: A payload of megafauna genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via a megafauna genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenetic.label: megafauna genome
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenomeExcavator.description:
A simplified genome extraction device designed to be usable by even the most simple-minded goon, as such it resembles a caulking gun that works in reverse. The best genetic material can be found in the brain and the automated, single-use design instantly compresses this material into a usable genome.

The desired subject needs to be docile and trusting of the user who intends on scooping out their brain in a process that is not-quite instantaneous. It is recommended to anesthetize the creature as any sudden movement on their part will interrupt the whole process. Alternatively, it will also work on a dead body...

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This excavator can be used to acquire the following genomes:
LanguageData.GR_MegafaunaGenomeExcavator.label: megafauna genome excavator
LanguageData.GR_MiniatureThrumboHorn.description: A bionic implant adding a small thrumbo horn where the creature's nose used to be. This implant acts as a nose, allowing the creature to breathe and smell, and can be used by the creature in melee. Birds, insects, and creatures that already have a thrumbo horn don't have enough space on their skull to install this implant.
LanguageData.GR_MiniatureThrumboHorn.label: animal Implant: miniature thrumbo horn
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.description:
Far too independent to be a combat animal but certainly can both dish out and take pain. Works best in the background, minding its own business and digging up metals it finds. No one is really sure why but it probably has to do with those massive whiskers.

A rodent hybrid with recessive ursine genes. An independent predator with a very low metabolism and high toughness. Has an unusual behaviour where it’ll periodically dig for treasures, typically raw metals.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.label: molebear
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Molebear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Molehorse.description:
An oversized naked mole-rat with frighteningly large chompers, however they’re not as dangerous as they look as they are quite timid and use said teeth for chewing up tough vegetables. Objectively cute when you give them a good brushing, but have a nasty habit of overshooting hand-fed snacks and biting on hands due to poor eyesight. A saddle is recommended for riding, as there is little fat and no fur to cushion their boney structure and loose skin.

An equine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Quite small and lacking in durability, but surprisingly capable as a mount. Exceedingly filthy for an animal that eats so little, however this is tied to its rapid gestation and faint regenerative capabilities.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Molehorse.label: molehorse
LanguageData.GR_Molehorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Molehorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Molehorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.description:
A hulking monstrosity that is genuinely caring for those around them, taking great joy and actual personal pleasure through working diligently and caring for those it considers friends and family. Evidently, it was bred specifically by megacorporations to work in dangerous mines, because human greed never changes.

A rodent hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Very low hunger rate and very intelligent, these gentle giants enjoy digging with their hands. They will sometimes work mining orders given the chance but they’re very slow due to their bulk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Molemen will periodically obey commands to mine. This will also work with the Quarry mod.
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.label: moleman
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Moleman.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MoleratNerveDampener.description: A biochip that reduces pain sensitivity, allowing the subject to survive pain shock.
LanguageData.GR_MoleratNerveDampener.label: human Implant: mole-rat nerve dampener
LanguageData.GR_MonitorBank.description: These monitors will never show any information again.
LanguageData.GR_MonitorBank.label: ancient monitor banks
LanguageData.GR_MonitorLizardBrain.description: A bionic implant using monitor lizard brain tissue. This highly intelligent reptile has very quick reflexes.
LanguageData.GR_MonitorLizardBrain.label: animal Implant: monitor lizard brain
LanguageData.GR_MothTympanalOrgan.description: A bionic implant adding a moth tympanal organ to the creature's ear. Moths can hear sounds of a higher frequency than any bat can produce.
LanguageData.GR_MothTympanalOrgan.label: animal Implant: moth tympanal organ
LanguageData.GR_MousePheromones.description: A bionic vomeronasal organ that allows the subject pheromonal communication with other animals, increasing the chance of taming and training them successfully.
LanguageData.GR_MousePheromones.label: human Implant: mouse pheromones
LanguageData.GR_Muffalobear.description:
An absolute unit of a muffalo hybrid with muscles bursting underneath the layers of fur and fat. An excellent caravan companion for those wanting to transport lots of goods and hot muffalobear milk makes living in eternal winter seem almost worth it.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive ursine genes. While they are slower and eat more compared to a regular muffalo, they are better at self defence and offer the highest carry capacity of all primary muffalo hybrids. They also produce good thick wool and delicious yet simple milk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalobear.label: muffalobear
LanguageData.GR_Muffalobear.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MuffalobearMilk.description: A delicious milk with roasted overtones. Very pleasurable to eat, but not very nutritious.
LanguageData.GR_MuffalobearMilk.label: muffalobear milk
LanguageData.GR_Muffalocat.description:
Combining fierce independence with an animal suited for travelling long distances might not have been the best idea. But critics have clearly never been nuzzled by a giant cat only to be buried alive in a pile of insulating hair in the winter, so what do they know.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive feline genes. Not the best for caravan duty but their regular grooming produces bundles of thick wool and can be milked for a rather disgusting milk filled with hair. A low maintenance way of getting more hair than you bargained for.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool without needing to be sheared. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalocat.label: muffalocat
LanguageData.GR_Muffalocat.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Muffalocat.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Muffalocat.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Muffalochicken.description:
Don’t let its adorable appearance fool you, this beastie is ready to fly off its handle at a moment's notice. The more it’s hurt, the more pissed off it gets and the faster it’ll try to rip its enemies to shreds.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive avian genes. A bit too slow and weak for a caravan companion but it makes for a vicious and suicidal protective companion. Produces a thick, chunky milk that tastes like a protein shake.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The melee cooldown of its attacks scales with the percentage of lost health, up to 6 times faster. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalochicken.label: muffalochicken
LanguageData.GR_Muffalochicken.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Muffalochicken.tools.1.label: beak
LanguageData.GR_MuffalochickenMilk.description: This lumpy milk seems to have concentrated protein chunks, tasting like chicken nuggets. Disgusting, but colonists seem to like it.
LanguageData.GR_MuffalochickenMilk.label: muffalochicken milk
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloGenetic.description:
A payload of muffalo genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All muffalo hybrid animals are pack animals, with primary muffalo hybrids getting 50% increased capacity caravaning. All muffalo hybrids have an increase to minimum comfortable temperature.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloGenetic.label: muffalo genome
LanguageData.GR_Muffalohorse.description:
Belligerent bulls with a penchant for the tastes of the wild wastes. A herd of these creatures can be occasionally seen trawling through regions, consuming practically all edible vegetation and leaving fertile waste in their path. Some argue that this is the ideal form of muffalo for nomad tribes, as they produce everything they need to survive winters and are large enough to feed said tribe through the harshest of seasons.

An equine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Very unusual in that it doesn’t help a caravan speed along, largely due to it excessively foraging and eating various vegetation from new locations, as well as roughage to help with digestion. The benefit from this behaviour is that this stimulation is the only way it’ll produce milk and wool, so herds of these hybrids can be taken on a long journey to provide produce vital to small and starting colonies.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalohorse.label: muffalohorse
LanguageData.GR_Muffalohorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Muffalohorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Muffalohorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.description:
Milking snakes for venom in order to create medicine is an extremely dangerous career. While this particular beast of burden proved to be capable in other areas, it was mainly synthesised in order to provide a safer way of sourcing an anaesthetic coagulant to be used in emergencies. Or someone was trying to get high by milking a reptilian space cow, who really knows.

A reptile hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A strong pack animal with a massive appetite and a proportionate filth rate. Quite durable and will prove a fatal mistake for any animal that attempts to hunt them, as their fangs, horns and claws are all venomous. Can be milked every ten days for a vial of paralysing coagulant, best used for large animals that are suffering from multiple lacerations.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.label: muffalokomodo
LanguageData.GR_Muffalokomodo.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloLung.description: An implant that uses muffalo genes to increase breathing capacity.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloLung.label: animal Implant: muffalo lung
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMammaries.description: By implanting muffalo mammaries into a colonist... Wait, what??? Who came up with this idea????? Anyway... this implant allows your colonist to be milked... What the...
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMammaries.label: human Implant: muffalo mammaries
LanguageData.GR_Muffaloman.description:
The animal we’ve all been waiting for, namely one that not only cleans up after itself but for others as well. Their fur is surprisingly soft and has antibacterial properties, which it uses along with its fingered paws to clean surfaces. Once the fur starts to become matted is when it needs to be trimmed.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Quite intelligent but they lose out on a lot of their progenitor’s hardiness against the cold. They are also very clean animals and will clean areas around the colony, and can be sheared regularly for a decent amount of wool.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans. Muffalomen are clean muffalo hybrids that will periodically clean around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Muffaloman.label: muffaloman
LanguageData.GR_Muffaloman.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMuscles.description: By weaving muffalo muscle strands with the subjects muscular system, strength, resilience and carrying capacity are greatly increased.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloMuscles.label: human Implant: muffalo strengthened muscles
LanguageData.GR_Muffalope.description:
A very naughty hybrid that can be told to relieve itself on command, creating puddles of chemfuel-infused urine around it. For those who just want to watch the world burn in the most vulgar way.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. A bit too slow for a caravan companion but make up for it with intelligence and an unusual chemfuel ability that lends itself for defence.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalope.label: muffalope
LanguageData.GR_Muffalope.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Muffalopede.description:
An oversized grub with udders that extrude jelly instead of milk, its hard-headedness serves it well in both getting what it wants and nutting anyone foolish enough to think they’re better than them. Allegedly there was a case where the owner was prevented from shearing the creature for so long that the overlapping plates completely prevented it from moving, who was afterwards thanked with a kick in the nether region.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Higher metabolism compared to other insectoid hybrids as well as stubborn, any predator foolish enough to think of them as an easy meal will be responded with a headbutt like a sledgehammer. Can work as a pack animal but refuses to haul comparatively heavy loads. Regularly produces precious jelly and can be sheared for chitin, which overlaps and protects the animal well from blunt attacks.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalopede.label: muffalopede
LanguageData.GR_Muffalopede.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloPelt.description: Cool! You can now make your own Game of Thrones / Rimworld cosplay! Or it can also be repurposed as a comfy rug.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloPelt.label: muffalo pelt
LanguageData.GR_Muffalorat.description:
Breeding a muffalo hybrid into such a small package means they have very little capacity for either wool or milk. They’re very quick though, and your handlers better be quicker on their daily milking and shearing.
