A muffalo hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Very weak as a pack animal but they breed quickly and can be milked and sheared daily. The amount of product is small, but it’s thick wool and nutritious milk.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalorat.label: muffalorat
LanguageData.GR_Muffalorat.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloRug.description: A muffalo pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloRug.label: muffalo pelt rug
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSkin.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a measure of defense but, more importantly, makes the subject more resistant to low temperatures.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSkin.label: human Implant: muffalo skin
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSpine.description: A spine implant that uses genetic splicing with muffalo genes to give an old (or damaged) spine decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy spines.
LanguageData.GR_MuffaloSpine.label: animal Implant: muffalo spine
LanguageData.GR_Muffalowolf.description:
Someone clearly wanted multiple muffalos that would be capable of tearing foolish attackers to shreds. They eat a ton but those massive teeth of theirs aren’t just good at chewing up vegetation. Plus they’re incredibly cute when they wag their massive tails.

A muffalo hybrid with recessive canine genes. The combination of lethality, speed and high intelligence makes this hybrid a fierce pack animal, both figuratively and literally. Capable of pack tactics with other muffalowolves that further increase their melee dodge, hit chance and dps.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> The pack tactics ability will give any muffalowolves around bonuses to melee dps, melee dodge and melee hit chance. All primary muffalo hybrids have 150% carrying capacity on caravans.
LanguageData.GR_Muffalowolf.label: muffalowolf
LanguageData.GR_Muffalowolf.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.description: This abomination is part chicken, part sentient cactus. If cactus nuggets weren't a thing, they are now. You can think of them as solar-powered egg layers.\n\nUseful abilities: Needlechicken are decent egg layers. They can defend themselves with tiny poison barb attacks, and they almost don't need sustenance (except for a large amount of sunlight).
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.label: needlechicken
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.tools.0.label: stings
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Needlechicken.verbs.0.label: tiny venomous barb
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggFertilized.descriptio: A fertilized needlechicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into a needlechick. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggFertilized.label: needlechicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggUnfertilized.descript: An unfertilized needlechicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_NeedlechickenEggUnfertilized.label: needlechicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_NeuronReinforcement.description: A biochip using animal stem cells to reinforce the natural abilities of human neurons, increasing learning and research speed.
LanguageData.GR_NeuronReinforcement.label: human Implant: neuron reinforcement
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.description: The nighthrumbo is a fearsome predator. Armed with strong jaws and its horn, as well as ranged quill attacks, it will make quick work of any prey it decides to attack. It is also a nocturnal animal, sleeping during the day and being active at night.\n\nUseful abilities: Ranged quill attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.label: nighthrumbo
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.1.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.2.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Nighthrumbo.verbs.0.label: huge quill
LanguageData.GR_NutrientVat.description: A slurry of liquefied organic pulp fills this reinforced glass vat. This solution is used by genetic experiments, and increases work speed when placed near a Vanilla Genetics Expanded workbench. One workbench can use to up to two nutrient vats.
LanguageData.GR_NutrientVat.label: nutrient vat
LanguageData.GR_Ovipositor.description: You know you have gone too far in your scientific experiments when you are implanting a human being with an ovipositor. This will enable the colonist to lay chicken eggs twice a day. It hurts as hell, though. Also, yuck.
LanguageData.GR_Ovipositor.label: human Implant: ovipositor
LanguageData.GR_Paraceramuffalo.description: The Paraceratherium was the biggest land mammal to ever exist. When you hibridize it with a sturdy muffalo, the resulting creature is the most powerful pack animal ever created. Incredibly stubborn and bad-tempered, though.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool and milk, and acts as an incredibly strong -albeit slow- pack beast.
LanguageData.GR_Paraceramuffalo.label: paraceramuffalo
LanguageData.GR_Paraceramuffalo.tools.0.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Paraceramuffalo.tools.1.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBear.description: The absolute alpha of units, the chonkiest of bois, winner of consecutive Fat Bear Weeks before being disqualified due to winning too often.\n\nA perfect ursine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon has a particularly powerful stun attack and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBear.label: ursine paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBoomalope.description: While more of a risk to keep around, they are considerably more efficient and don’t eat your crops as much. Plus their permanent sad expression just makes you want to pet them, how can you say no.\n\nA perfect boomalope animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon produces deepchem instead and explodes on death in a much more violent fashion.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBoomalope.label: boomalope paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonBoomalope.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChicken.description: Sure the little blighter is more stubborn than its progenitor, but not having to gather the eggs so often and a bigger fried egg makes all the difference.\n\nA perfect avian animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon possesses inherent wildness not present in domesticated chickens but is more likely to stay put.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChicken.label: avian paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChicken.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggFertilized.descripti: Fertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness. If all goes well, it should hatch into an avian paragon.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggFertilized.label: avian paragon egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggUnfertilized.descrip: Unfertilized avian paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. These eggs are pure, pristine, perfect examples of chicken eggs. Immaculate eggness.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonChickenEggUnfertilized.label: avian paragon egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonFeline.description:
An incredible, large fur baby that not only wants you to scratch its belly, but regularly and without fear of getting your eyes gouged out by their massive claws. Also they’re smart enough to go after prey less than half their size, but that’s more so they don’t have to spend so much time grooming themselves afterwards.

A perfect feline animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is incredibly intelligent and can be properly trained with minimal retraining, as well as being an excellent and affectionate companion.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Cat reflexes ability: twice per day, it can use an ability that gives it further melee capabilities.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonFeline.label: feline paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonFeline.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonFeline.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonFeline.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHorse.description: The most majestic of horses is proud and magnificent, and doesn’t care for your rules, man. So keep them properly penned up, because they’ll try to escape to freedom so they can run wild and free. And then wonder where all the carrots, sugar cubes and salt licks have disappeared to.\n\nA perfect equine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon makes for an excellent mount and pack animal for caravans.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHorse.label: equine paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHorse.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHorse.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHumanoid.description:
There is a saying that all of life is a circle, and that eventually everything returns to its origins only to begin again. This particular humanoid has undergone some mental evolution while at the same time suffering from physical devolution, resembling more closely humanity’s distant evolutionary ancestors. Just don’t say monkey.

A perfect humanoid animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon can drive man’s curiosity ever forward by providing a boost to research speed with a well-placed insightful word of wisdom.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid paragons will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Research drive ability: offers investigative insights to nearby researches, allowing them to more easily reach a breakthrough by increasing research speed by 10%.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHumanoid.label: humanoid paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHumanoid.tools.0.label: left fist
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHumanoid.tools.1.label: right fist
LanguageData.GR_ParagonHumanoid.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.description: Small and venomous, with a deadly fart, not quite what one was expecting for the perfect reptilian form. I mean, you’d think you’d get a tyrannosaurus rex or something but no, it seems that the humble iguana is here to stay. If anything, the shock of something so small moving so fast has got to be worth something.\n\nA perfect reptile animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is oviparous and can manually rupture neck sacs containing venomous gas.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.label: reptile paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguana.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized reptile paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a reptile paragon.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggFertilized.label: reptile paragon egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized reptile paragon egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonIguanaEggUnfertilized.label: reptile paragon egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.description:
A terrifying callback to the original Sorne geneline, this mobile hive was originally designed to engage the mechanoid enemy head-on. By carrying an entire colony of insects with hardened mandibles, their ability to practically disassemble the mechanical menace proved just as effective at turning against their human overlords.

A perfect insectoid animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon is extremely intelligent and carries a dangerous brood of warrior flies. A burst of pheromones summons the drones to protect their hive and queen, shredding apart all other nearby creatures.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them. Insect clouds ability: twice a day it can create insect clouds around it that damage any animal crossing them, except insectoid paragons, which are immune.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.label: insectoid paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonInsectoid.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.description: A return to simpler times, when everyone could have sworn blind that muffalos used to be able to produce milk but were instead fooled by hornless paragons.\n\nA perfect muffalo animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon also produces milk and can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.label: muffalo paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonMuffalo.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.description: Look, I know what you’re thinking. Why is the rat the paragon of all rodents? And even if you’re not thinking that, this is rhetorical anyway so there. What other rodent has caused civilization to collapse due to poorly held beliefs and lack of proper hygiene. As such, we give you the King Rat. Or Queen Rat, if you want a whole colony showing up.\n\nA perfect rodent animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon has a very fast gestation period and its attacks transmit the plague.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.label: rodent paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonRat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.description: The gigantic, graceful creature has somehow been improved even further, proving infallibly that there’s always a taller mountain. Significantly easier to train and with a more efficient digestive system, this creature is possibly how the thrumbo originally was when first bred in a lab before it got tainted through terraforming.\n\nA perfect colossal animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon makes for an excellent mount for caravans and can forcibly rush itself, getting faster and creating dust clouds that impede ranged attacks.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.label: colossal paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_ParagonThrumbo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.description: An improved version of man’s best friend, easier to train, more assertive and dangerous to those that threaten a household. Sadly, far too proud to admit it really wants a belly rub.\n\nA perfect canine animal, paragon of all similar animals with no needless junk genetic data. Besides the various improvements to the baseline animal’s abilities, this paragon can be slaughtered for a valuable pelt, which can either be used for decoration or sold.
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.label: canine paragon
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_ParagonWolf.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParaMilk.description: Paraceramuffalo milk is rich and creamy. Probably too rich in fat, but certainly tasty.
LanguageData.GR_ParaMilk.label: paraceramuffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_Parasaurolope.description: This hybrid ornithopod has two cranial crests, which it uses in mating rituals. Their whole body is covered in large sacs of volatile chemicals.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> This animal was created with one goal in mind: to produce huge amounts of chemfuel. Their corpses can be directly refined into compressed chemfuel in a biorefinery.
LanguageData.GR_Parasaurolope.label: parasaurolope
LanguageData.GR_Parasaurolope.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ParasaurolopeEggFertilized.descriptio: Fertilized parasaurolope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a parasaurolope.
LanguageData.GR_ParasaurolopeEggFertilized.label: parasaurolope egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ParasaurolopeEggUnfertilized.descript: Unfertilized parasaurolope egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ParasaurolopeEggUnfertilized.label: parasaurolope egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_PlasmaBurst.label: plasma burst
LanguageData.GR_Poison_Cloud.label: poison cloud
LanguageData.GR_Poison_Gas.label: poison gas
LanguageData.GR_PoisonAmpoule.description: This tiny vial is filled with a concentrated solution of Muffalokomodo saliva, which acts as a powerful neuromuscular-blocking drug. This effectively paralyses anyone that drinks it, though it is not lethal. It will also stop bleeding rate while active, perhaps allowing a person to avoid bleeding to death if the poison is administered quick enough. Effects wear off after 6 hours.
LanguageData.GR_PoisonAmpoule.label: poison ampoule
LanguageData.GR_PoisonBlast.label: poison blast
LanguageData.GR_PoisonBreath.label: poison breath
LanguageData.GR_Proj_ThrownSac.label: thrown sac
LanguageData.GR_PsychicDampeners.description: Artificial hearing aids that block psychic emanations, providing considerable resistance to mechanoids' psychic attacks.
LanguageData.GR_PsychicDampeners.label: human Implant: psychic dampeners
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.description:
For a combination of two relatively small animals, the rabbitcat is fucking massive and has a robust physique. It knows no fear and will tackle anything it wants to eat. Some say this was the other attempt to create the Rabbit of Caerbannog, because who says geneticists don’t have a sick sense of humour.

A rodent hybrid with recessive feline genes. Very intelligent considering the source material, these hybrids are fast, lethal and possess a robust physique with rapid natural regenerative capabilities. Quite stubborn so they require frequent, competent training but they aren’t afraid to show their appreciation.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.label: rabbitcat
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.description:
Legends say of an ancient and vicious monster, a creature so foul, so cruel that no man yet has fought with it and lived. This was a somewhat successful attempt, as its weakness lulls the enemy into a false sense of security so that it can rip their throat out. So do you feel lucky, punk?

A rodent hybrid with recessive avian genes. A tiny, weak, fast-moving pest with an insane appetite for its size. Incredibly fast gestation period and a 1% chance to instantly kill when attacking.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.label: rabbitchicken
LanguageData.GR_Rabbitchicken.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Rack.description: This server rack once housed a lot of genetic information, but all its data has now degraded completely.
LanguageData.GR_Rack.label: ancient server rack
LanguageData.GR_RandomBooster.label: random booster
LanguageData.GR_RandomExcavator.label: random excavator
LanguageData.GR_RandomGenoframe.label: random genoframe
LanguageData.GR_RandomGenome.label: random genome
LanguageData.GR_Raptortoise.description: This raptor theropod has strange turtle traits. Why strange? Well, if you picture an armored dinosaur you tend to imagine something like an ankylosaurus, not a raptor with a tortoise shell on its back.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Raptortoise.label: raptortoise
LanguageData.GR_Raptortoise.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Raptortoise.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Raptortoise.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggFertilized.description: Fertilized raptortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a raptortoise.
LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggFertilized.label: raptortoise egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized raptortoise egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_RaptortoiseEggUnfertilized.label: raptortoise egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.description:
An interesting pack animal that packs an incredible punch that also pisses and shits everywhere. That ain’t even the worst part, because these rodents of unusual size are also plague carriers with complete immunity to toxicity. Sometimes scientists just can’t leave well enough alone.

A rodent hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Quite clever, almost absurdly lethal and quick on its feet. While they are herbivores, they will still happily munch on corpses thanks to their large appetite. Not as short a gestation period compared to other primary rodent hybrids, but every bite transmits the plague.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.label: ratffalo
LanguageData.GR_Ratffalo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_RatMilk.description: This milk has a rich, thick quality to it. It's origins, however, might be a bit dubious... Unless you trust mafia guys called Tony.
LanguageData.GR_RatMilk.label: ratffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_RazorProjectile.label: razor scythe
LanguageData.GR_ReptileGenetic.description:
A payload of reptile genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 2 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have greatly increased minimum and maximum comfortable temperature. They also have melee and ranged venomous attacks.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_ReptileGenetic.label: reptile genome
LanguageData.GR_RodentEar.description: An ear implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) ear decent hearing. This implant is not meant to replace healthy ears.
LanguageData.GR_RodentEar.label: animal Implant: rodent ear
LanguageData.GR_RodentGenetic.description:
A payload of rodent genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have a reduced body size, but greatly increased reproduction rates (if fertile).

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_RodentGenetic.label: rodent/leporid genome
LanguageData.GR_RodentNose.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with rodent genes to give an old (or damaged) nose decent smelling. This implant is not meant to replace healthy noses. Can't be installed on birds and insects.
LanguageData.GR_RodentNose.label: animal Implant: rodent nose
LanguageData.GR_ROM_RawCHFood_FormlessMeat.descriptio: Raw meat from a formless spawn of Tsathoggua. Or... or at least a boomalope abomination that *looked* similar. Hard to know with these things.
LanguageData.GR_ROM_RawCHFood_FormlessMeat.label: formless spawn meat
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybrid_Antenna.descriptio: A collapsed antenna that is completely unable of transmitting anything ever again.
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybrid_Antenna.label: ancient mechahybrid antenna
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybridizer.description: The broken maws of this ancient factory will never be able to close again.
LanguageData.GR_Ruined_Mechahybridizer.label: ancient mechahybridizer
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientBioBattery.description: This ancient tank once converted biological matter into an electrical current. It is now completely broken and useless.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientBioBattery.label: ancient ruined bio-battery tank
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientGeneTailoringPod.descrip: This ancient structure was once used to inject nanomachines in a subject in an attempt to create the perfect soldier. It now lies rusted and unsalvageable.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAncientGeneTailoringPod.label: ancient ruined gene-tailoring pod
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalControlHub.description: It seems that this structure was capable of emitting some kind of electromagnetic radiation used to control brain function of certain animals. This was crude and primitive technology prone to failure and instability, and as such it is completely useless now.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalControlHub.label: ancient animal control hub
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalEnrichmentCenter.descript: Whatever was held inside this dank, rusted metal coffin has long since escaped. It is damaged beyond all repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedAnimalEnrichmentCenter.label: ancient animal enrichment center
LanguageData.GR_RuinedArchotechPlatform.description: This deteriorated platform seems to have been used to assemble some kind of machinery. It now lays bare and empty.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedArchotechPlatform.label: ancient construction platform
LanguageData.GR_RuinedBarrelDouble.description: Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedBarrelDouble.label: ancient ruined lab barrels
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneDuplicator.description: This semi-spherical vat seems to have held some kind of viscuous liquid inside, as evidenced by the rancid-smelling residue coating its insides. It is now completely ruined and only useful as metal scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneDuplicator.label: ancient gene duplicator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenePod.description: An ancient genepod that was used to combine genetic material. This machine is obsolete and damaged beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenePod.label: ancient ruined genepod
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneRecombinator.description: A completely obsolete model of gene recombinator, that is also deteriorated and rusted beyond and salvaging.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneRecombinator.label: ancient gene recombinator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticDatabase.description: This ancient computer was used to storage genetic information and feed genetic dataprints to other machines, but its components have deteriorated so far that all the data it contained has degraded to nothingness.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticDatabase.label: ancient genetic database
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticExtractionTable.descript: Yet another workstation that has deteriorated beyond all recovery. A foul-smelling vial of stagnant liquid warns you to keep away from it.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticExtractionTable.label: ancient genetic extraction table
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticStorage.description: This little wheeled table housed a small specialized computer and refrigerated trays to store genetic material, but it is now so damaged it can only be used as scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGeneticStorage.label: ancient genetic storage
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenomeSequencer.description: A large centrifuge used in gene sequencing. Its corroded parts will never be of use to anyone again.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedGenomeSequencer.label: ancient genome sequencer
LanguageData.GR_RuinedHexagelPlant.label: ancient hexagel core reactor
LanguageData.GR_RuinedIncubator.description: This small canister has been smashed to pieces and whatever it contained has long evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedIncubator.label: ancient ruined incubator
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabBarrel.description: Whatever this barrel held has long ago evaporated.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabBarrel.label: ancient ruined lab barrel
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabShelf.description: Rusted shelving units coated in grime and dust.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedLabShelf.label: ancient lab shelf
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMechanoidTinkeringTable.descrip: This workbench appears to have been used to try and reverse engineer mechanoid components, but it is damaged to the point of uselessness.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMechanoidTinkeringTable.label: ancient mechanoid tinkering table
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMutaniteCentrifuge.description: A machine used to process mutagenic minerals, now completely broken and unsalvageable.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedMutaniteCentrifuge.label: ancient mutanite centrifuge
LanguageData.GR_RuinedNutrientVat.description: A slurry of liquefied organic pulp filled this reinforced glass vat. It now lays broken and empty.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedNutrientVat.label: ancient nutrient vat
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulper.description: A weird-looking machine that shredded wood into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulper.label: ancient pulper
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulpRecycler.description: An obsolete, ancient machine that recycled wood, corpses and bones into organic pulp. Broken beyond repair.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedPulpRecycler.label: ancient pulp recycler
LanguageData.GR_RuinedRefrigeratedCoils.description: These structural cryofuel coils have been completely ruined by time and neglect. They can no longer function.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedRefrigeratedCoils.label: ancient refrigerated coils
LanguageData.GR_RuinedTissueGrowingVat.description: A metal sarcophagus used to grow bio-mechanic implants in a controlled manner. Its machinery has been gutted and has later decayed into rusted scrap.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedTissueGrowingVat.label: ancient tissue growing vat
LanguageData.GR_RuinedVials.description: Small canisters that once held some valuable liquids.
LanguageData.GR_RuinedVials.label: ancient ruined vials
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallA.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallA.label: rusted laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallB.description: An impassable wall. Capable of holding up a roof.
LanguageData.GR_RustedWallB.label: rusted laboratory wall
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearPelt.description: This enormous bear pelt is part hair and part scales. It is probably valuable as a trade good. Or it can also be repurposed as a huge rug.
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearPelt.label: scaly bear pelt
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearRug.description: Really, couldn't you decorate with vinyl decals, like a normal, non-insane person? This looks horrendous. Some of the colonists seem to like it, though..
LanguageData.GR_ScalyBearRug.label: scaly bear rug
LanguageData.GR_ScytherTail.description: A scyther tail created by attaching a jury-rigged scyther blade to an animal's tail. Note that only creatures with a natural tail can use this.
LanguageData.GR_ScytherTail.label: animal Implant: scyther tail
LanguageData.GR_Shiftskin.label: formless boomalope shiftskin
LanguageData.GR_SignA.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignA.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignB.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignB.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignC.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignC.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignD.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignD.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignE.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignE.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignF.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignF.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignG.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignG.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_SignH.description: A weathered sign advertising a company that has long been forgotten.
LanguageData.GR_SignH.label: ancient sign
LanguageData.GR_Sink.description: Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...
LanguageData.GR_Sink.label: ancient sink
LanguageData.GR_SmallBioLight.description: A metallic capsule filled with some bioluminescent compound. This one seems to still be producing some faint light.
LanguageData.GR_SmallBioLight.label: ancient bioluminescent light
LanguageData.GR_SmokeBomb.label: smokepop shell
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.description:
Possibly one of the weirdest and cutest snakes to be ever created. They have several tiny legs which are used to push its body along, while also having an invitingly soft fur. Quite a lot of people never stop freaking out from something sliding up the backs of their legs only to get softly headbutted by a purring python.

A reptile hybrid with recessive feline genes. An unusually furry snake that enjoys nuzzling while also hunting for prey much weaker than itself. Incredibly intelligent and easy to train, they combine quick reflexes and an incredibly nasty bite and spitting attack.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.label: snakecat
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Snakecat.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_SniperFlechette.label: sniper flechette
LanguageData.GR_Spidercat.description:
People really never understand what it is to have a bad hair day, and this feisty feline is gonna help teach them. As for those who don’t want to be covered in hairballs formed from silk strands and adhesive mucus, them’s the brakes, kid. Don’t wanna get spat on, then be friends with them and get over your arachnophobia.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive feline genes. Both quick and decent offensive power, this one can handle themselves in both melee combat and a blinding ranged attack. A little difficult to train, but very clever and affectionate with flexible scales to resist cuts.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Spidercat.label: spidercat
LanguageData.GR_Spidercat.tools.0.label: mandibles
LanguageData.GR_Spidercat.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Spidercat.verbs.0.label: blinding spit
LanguageData.GR_Spiderhorse.description:
Arachnophobia is no joke for many people, and the researcher that created this armoured arachnid had an inherent desire to share their fear with others. Granted they passed out, frothing at the mouth, when the creature emerged from the pod, but damn it it’s hard to argue with results in a riding companion that can not only keep up with the competition, but also disable them and eat them on the go as a tasty snack.

An equine hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. A rather slow and filthy hybrid of this category, but it works very well as a ride due to the multiple legs easing the burden of long distance travel inherent to caravaning. However, their main advantage is that they are also incredibly capable in combat, being armour plated and capable of both high damage and ranged stunning attacks by shooting globs of sticky threads.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Spiderhorse.label: spiderhorse
LanguageData.GR_Spiderhorse.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Spiderhorse.verbs.0.label: web
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.description:
Neutroamine is rather difficult to manufacture due to the sterile environment needed in creating a synthetic precursor chemical. So some insane madman decided to create a living creature that can synthesise its own neutroamine. Granted it’s not a lot, but have enough of these disturbingly powerful and quick cobras and no one will ever try to rob you, or live long enough to get away.

A reptile hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. For an animal so small, it has an incredibly powerful bite and can spit venom. Sadly they can’t be trained but will happily hunt smaller prey that happen to wander into their territory or fight back against any foolish predators. Can be milked once every several days for a vial of neutroamine, so they function well as part of a large drug farm.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.label: spidersnake
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Spidersnake.verbs.0.label: poison spit
LanguageData.GR_Spinobear.description: This hybrid therapod has bear-like characteristics. There are several theories concerning the use of the Spinosaur's spinal fan-sail, but in the case of the Spinobear it is clear what use it has: kicking your ass.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All bear hybrids have stun attacks. Aside from obviously being a very powerful creature, they produce a weird mixture of wool and scales that can be sheared and used in textile operations. They can also be slaughtered for their pelts. Like all dinosaur hybrids, they are oviparous.
LanguageData.GR_Spinobear.label: spinobear
LanguageData.GR_Spinobear.tools.0.label: left leg
LanguageData.GR_Spinobear.tools.1.label: right leg
LanguageData.GR_Spinobear.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggFertilized.description: Fertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into an spinobear.
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggFertilized.label: spinobear egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized spinobear egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_SpinobearEggUnfertilized.label: spinobear egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.description:
It’s small, it’s cute, it’s a ticking time bomb waiting for some idiot to think it looks delicious. It’s not going to be a big explosion, but that doesn’t matter much when it’s in your mouth.

A rodent hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. Small, weak but fast, this hybrid suits those looking for a fuel production method in a small package. Produces chemfuel much more efficiently compared to a boomalope but it needs to be milked every day.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All rodent hybrids have fast gestation, as long as a fertility unblocker was used when creating them in the genomorpher.
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.label: squirralope
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Squirralope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_StabilizedFertilityUnblocker.descript:
Compressed medicine that allows hybrids to develop functional sexual organs. Effectively allows hybrids to reproduce with the exact same species.

This booster can't be crafted, only scavenged.

Note: Insectoid hybrids are always incapable of reproducing.
LanguageData.GR_StabilizedFertilityUnblocker.label: stabilized fertility unblocker
LanguageData.GR_Stegothrumbo.description: A colossal, armored dinosaur-thrumbo hybrid. Probably not as smart as a thrumbo, but it makes up for it by being incredibly tough.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Greater stamina ability: twice per day, the Stegothrumbo can become almost impervious to damage.
LanguageData.GR_Stegothrumbo.label: stegothrumbo
LanguageData.GR_Stegothrumbo.tools.0.label: tail
LanguageData.GR_Stegothrumbo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggFertilized.description: Fertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive! If all goes well, it should hatch into a stegothrumbo.
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggFertilized.label: stegothrumbo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized stegothrumbo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. This is insanely nutritive!
LanguageData.GR_StegothrumboEggUnfertilized.label: stegothrumbo egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_StunBolt.label: stun bolt
LanguageData.GR_TastyMeat.description: Raw meat from a manchicken. Unbelievably tasty, and unbelievably unethical. Light, nutritive and with no poisoning chance.
LanguageData.GR_TastyMeat.label: crispy meat
LanguageData.GR_TemplateGenetic.description: Absolutely and completely worthless, but when properly processed alongside an actual genome it can be trimmed down to an identical copy.
LanguageData.GR_TemplateGenetic.label: template genome
LanguageData.GR_ThickHybridWool.description: Coarse, strong wool. Provides heat and cold protection, as well as decent defense.
LanguageData.GR_ThickHybridWool.label: thick hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_ThinHybridWool.description: Brittle, strange smelling wool. Not the best wool to craft apparel with, though it's better than nothing. Provides nice heat insulation.
LanguageData.GR_ThinHybridWool.label: thin hybrid wool
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.description:
Now here’s a creature you absolutely want to keep properly fed, and you’re going to need entire fields to support a single one. The ridiculous amount of chemfuel they produce means that the ceasing of their life functions is more accurately described as “thermonuclear”. So if they are going to blow up, send them as a surprise gift to friends and family who keep trying to kill you.

A colossal hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. The addition of chemfuel production has vastly increased the amount of food they need to survive, but they’re also smart enough to not need a pen. Consider keeping them far away from your growing fields if the local biome can grow vegetation fast enough to support one.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.label: thrumbalope
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbalope.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.description:
If you thought Thrumbos were tough and hit like a tank, may I present to you the answer to all your prayers. If you can feed the thing, this absolute unit would work extremely well as an assault force against any force foolish enough to threaten its feedstock. Plus you get to ride it on long journeys, possibly clearing entire forests on the way.

A colossal hybrid with recessive ursine genes. While proportionately it has a lower metabolism, its 50% increase in size still leaves it with a higher hunger rate than a thrumbo. They are however easier to train, hit much harder and can take even more punishment.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.label: thrumbear
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbear.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboBrain.description: A bionic brain implant using thrumbo neural tissue. Superb intelligence and reflexes.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboBrain.label: animal Implant: bionic thrumbo brain
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.description:
The other half of the super predator duo, along with the Thrumwolf, exchanging some ferocity and durability in exchange for going for the trachea and spitting suffocating hairballs. That might not sound that dangerous but imagine having a fistful of hair pulled from a shower drain smothering your mouth, and you’ll realise that’s still not disgusting enough. But for those that it appreciates, it will often pin them down and snuggle them given the chance.

A colossal hybrid with recessive feline genes. A large predator with an insane appetite with a focus on ferocious attacks that restrict their prey’s breathing. Extremely agile with a soft spot for those it loves, best suited for biomes that support enough wildlife to sate its hunger.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.label: thrumbocat
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbocat.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.description:
This is probably the closest chickens will be able to get to recovering their past glory as super predators. They’ll often hunt any other animal for their next meal, multiple times a day, and you haven’t yet lived until you’ve ridden one on a long journey. If they could be further bred to cross mountains and oceans, they’d be almost perfect.

A colossal hybrid with recessive avian genes. The combination of genes has turned this somewhat peaceful herbivore/dendrovore into a full carnivore, which makes it easier to support their large appetite. As an intelligent predator, it can move incredibly quickly for its size and its attacks are incredibly fierce. It also lays massive eggs that can feed entire families.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.label: thrumbochicken
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbochicken.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ThrumbochickenEggFertilized.descripti: Fertilized Thrumbochicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumbochickenEggFertilized.label: thrumbochicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_ThrumbochickenEggUnfertilized.descrip: Unfertilized Thrumbochicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumbochickenEggUnfertilized.label: thrumbochicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboEye.description: A bionic eye reinforced with specialized photoreceptors from a powerful thrumbo.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboEye.label: animal Implant: bionic thrumbo eye
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboGenetic.description:
A payload of colossal genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 3 genome can only be used as primary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Extreme increase in size, health, damage and market value at the cost of slower attack speed.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can only be extracted via an archotech genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboGenetic.label: colossal genome
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboJaws.description: A bionic implant adding thrumbo jaws to the creature's mouth. These creatures devour trees, so you can imagine their jaws are up to the task.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboJaws.label: animal Implant: bionic thrumbo jaws
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboLeg.description: A bionic animal leg reinforced with muscle tissue from a powerful elephant.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboLeg.label: animal Implant: elephant leg
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.description:
About as close as anyone has ever gotten in making a recreation of dragons from folklore. Capable of surviving in extreme cold or heat, the only thing really missing is a pair of wings and an innate desire to hoard piles of gold for a nest. That and the fact that it isn’t carnivorous, instead having an appetite for vegetables and wood fibres.

A colossal hybrid with recessive reptile genes. With a mouth dripping with venom and tough, flexible scales covering the body, this hybrid makes for a nasty and incredibly durable combat hybrid. If that wasn’t enough, it can also breath out a fog of noxious fumes and move deceptively quickly.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.label: thrumbolizard
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbolizard.verbs.0.label: poison breath
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.description:
Look, we know what you want, a thrumbo that won’t eat your crops before they’ve harvested, thereby wasting entire seasons of growth for your most valuable plants. And we’re here to tell you it doesn’t exist. But we’ve gotten close in that this one will typically harvest your plants before it eats them for worse nutritional gain. Unless it’s trees, in which case we got nothing.

A colossal hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Considerably easier to train and maintain, this particular hybrid will help around the colony by cutting and harvesting designated plants. Unusually lean for how much it eats, it won’t provide much in butchering.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Thrumbomen will obey orders to cut and harvest plants around the colony.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.label: thrumboman
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumboman.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.description:
Practically an overgrown and intelligent pest capable of tunnelling underground, so not even a nuke could clear an infestation. And it truly would be an infestation, as it has a gestation period measured in days rather than weeks, with a single breeding pair capable of wiping out an entire agricultural planet.

A colossal hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Incredibly durable and fast breeding, this surprisingly small hybrid can work as a durable screening force. One of the weakest colossal hybrids in terms of attack power but very few can outlast its incredibly robust physique.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Burrow underground: Thrumborats can burrow underground to make them almost immune to damage for a short while.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.label: thrumborat
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.2.label: left paw
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.3.label: right paw
LanguageData.GR_Thrumborat.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboSkin.description: A synthetic skin substitute that is much thicker and a much better insulator than human skin. This provides a lot of defense and makes the subject more resistant to extremes of temperature.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumboSkin.label: human Implant: thrumbo skin
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.description:
A leftover bioweapon from a now extinct civilisation designed to completely eradicate biomes with dense vegetation. Records indicate that the local indigineous tribes were very resistant to corporate takeover and the megacorp in charge wanted to reduce profit losses. Eventually the planet starved as they were unable to stop these creatures from feasting on farmlands and food reserves.

A colossal hybrid with recessive insectoid genes. A slow moving tank with limited regenerative capabilities, they are almost unkillable if it weren’t for their crippling hunger. As durable as a capital ship, any colony that could possibly support the diet of even a single one would be considered impervious to raids.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. They are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.label: thrumbospider
LanguageData.GR_Thrumbospider.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumdraeodon.description: A huge, boar-like animal that will eat anything on its path. Thrumdraedons are cunning and resourceful.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor. Keen senses ability: Twice a day, the Thrumdraeodon can activate an ability that gives it increased awareness of its surreoundings.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumdraeodon.label: thrumdraeodon
LanguageData.GR_Thrumdraeodon.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumdraeodon.tools.1.label: left hoof
LanguageData.GR_Thrumdraeodon.tools.2.label: right hoof
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.description:
One of the largest creatures in existence, dwarfing even the thrumbo and the mighty Gallatross. Capable of transporting a massive amount of material, producing a luxurious and excellent wool as well as thick, nutritious milk, they are almost the perfect farm animal. That is as long as you don’t look at your feed costs and die of a heart attack, but the scientists that designed this hybrid never had to worry about such things. Food is something for the proles to worry about.

A colossal hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. Completely herbivorous and with an appetite large enough to cause even large colonies to starve within a single season. This hybrid can prove worth the food costs as it produces thick, delicious milk every couple of days as well as luxurious wool every couple of seasons. Slightly more dimwitted than its progenitor and will attempt to wander off in search of food if not kept penned up.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.label: thrumffalo
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumffalo.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_ThrumffaloMilk.description: Milk from a thrumffalo. Very nutritive. Also with a sky-high cholesterol content, but in the Rim nobody judges you.
LanguageData.GR_ThrumffaloMilk.label: thrumffalo milk
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.description:
A very nice combination of two highly adaptive baseline creatures which arguably results in the best riding companion man could ask for. While this isn’t apparent in their speed, it definitely is so once a rider experiences the thundering beats of this horsey positively barreling through entire forests and marshland in order to earn its next sugar cube the size of a fist.

A colossal hybrid with recessive equine genes. While they have a lower muscle-to-fat ratio compared to their progenitor, the overall placement and shape of the horn vastly reduces their combat efficiency. Instead, they more than make up for this shortfall with an incredible endurance even compared to paragons. An excellent riding companion that is easier to train and can even carry an acceptable amount of luggage, the majority of which contains feed.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM. Always starts as draftable, even if not using a controller booster to create it. Charge ability: when drafted, the player can use the charge ability to make the hybrid jump towards a target and cause massive damage with its horn to a single random body part. The ability can be triggered every 2 hours.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.label: thrumhorse
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumhorse.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.description:
There’s a saying about the scientific community, namely “your scientists were so preoccupied with whether they could, they didn’t stop to think if they should”. This is entirely relevant to this creature, a super predator with practically no equal, a single one of these is enough to require an entire rainforest to support their daily sustenance. If that wasn’t bad enough, they were made along with the Thrumbocat as part of a duo.

A colossal hybrid with recessive canine genes. A large predator with an insane appetite with the necessary ferocity to support itself, very few colonies can support the diet of such a hybrid. Incredibly robust, fast and a terrifying ferocity, they will hunt multiple times a day and often go after prey much bigger than themselves.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All colossal hybrids have very slow attacks, but hit very hard, and possess natural armor.
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.label: thrumwolf
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.tools.0.label: horn
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.tools.2.label: left foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.tools.3.label: right foot
LanguageData.GR_Thrumwolf.tools.4.label: head
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrinder.description:
A workplace accident waiting to happen, the Tissue Grinder takes the corpses of animals and carefully rips and tears them to pieces in order to extract useful genetic data in the form of Genoframes. The hardware is also capable of combining two identical frames into a superior form by comparing and improving upon latent and inferior genetic material.

Workspeed is influenced by someone who actually knows what they’re looking for in order to skip some of the more routine tasks, and possibly shouldn’t be placed into the hands of someone with no concept of ethics, morality or conscience.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay notes:</color> Bear in mind that the cost in corpses effectively doubles for each quality level, making a legendary genoframe prohibitive. The main usefulness of these recipes is to convert two genoframes of a given quality to a higher one, but your main source of genoframes shouldn't be hand crafting them. Go out there and explore abandoned labs!
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrinder.label: tissue grinder
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrowingVat.description: This particular machine can be explained as a simplified and miniaturized version of a Genomorpher, using only a single genome which influences an inserted Genoframe. Instead of trying to create an entire animal, it instead creates very specific limbs or organs. Due to the vastly simplified process and minimal variables, no complications arise during the process and the resulting product is similarly influenced by the quality of the Genoframe.\n\nThe Implant Augmentator can be used to create implants for animals - not just hybrids - with the quality of Genoframe dictating the deficits or bonuses those implants provide. Not all implants are created equal, some being inferior replacements and others providing new capabilities provided the animal in question can support them. Human implants are also a possibility.
LanguageData.GR_TissueGrowingVat.label: implant augmentator
LanguageData.GR_Toilet.description: Drinking from this will give you more than just a few rads...
LanguageData.GR_Toilet.label: ancient toilet
LanguageData.GR_TrapIED_Boom.description: An improvised explosive trap built out of... best if you don't think about it. Cannot be placed adjacent to other traps. Animals can sense these when calm. Less potent than regular high-explosive shell traps.
LanguageData.GR_TrapIED_Boom.label: boom-pustule trap
LanguageData.GR_Triceraffalo.description: This enormous ceratopsid can be used as an incredibly powerful pack beast. It is also quite capable of defending itself, with its sheer mass and huge horns.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Produces wool, eggs, milk, and acts as a very powerful, though slow, pack beast.
LanguageData.GR_Triceraffalo.label: triceraffalo
LanguageData.GR_Triceraffalo.tools.0.label: horns
LanguageData.GR_Triceraffalo.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggFertilized.description: Fertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a triceraffalo.
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggFertilized.label: triceraffalo egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggUnfertilized.descripti: Unfertilized triceraffalo egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_TriceraffaloEggUnfertilized.label: triceraffalo egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.description:
A cheeky little shit that likes to go around and play with switches, to satisfy their curiosity much like how an ignorant child would. Perhaps a little too smart for their own good. Their behaviour is similar to failed service animals that learn how to operate household items without restraint.

An avian hybrid with recessive humanoid genes. Surprisingly hardy, quick and very intelligent, these creatures make for a particularly filthy and hungry egg-layer. Their little wings are quite dextrous and can be used to flick switches.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> Humanoid hybrids will periodically engage other colonists in conversation, talking about a variety of topics. This increases the social recreation needs of those they interact with by 10% (subject to diminishing returns like all joy types). If using Ideology, humanoid hybrids are subject to precepts. Turkeymen periodically flick switches around the colony (any building that can be toggled on / off by a colonist).
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.label: turkeyman
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.0.label: claws
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.1.label: toothed beak
LanguageData.GR_Turkeyman.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_TurkeymanEggFertilized.description: Fertilized turkeyman egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_TurkeymanEggFertilized.label: turkeyman egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_TurkeymanEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized turkeyman egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_TurkeymanEggUnfertilized.label: turkeyman egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_UrsineGenetic.description:
A payload of ursine genetic material. Can be used at the genomorpher to create a growth cell of a hybrid animal. This tier 1 genome can be used as either a primary or secondary genome.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All primary ursine hybrids have stunning attacks, and high body size.

<color=#E5E54C>Extraction:</color> Can be extracted via the genome extractor table or any genome excavator from:
LanguageData.GR_UrsineGenetic.label: ursine genome
LanguageData.GR_Velociwolf.description: This raptor theropod hybrid escaped the shame of having feathers by instead having hair. So, uhm, still ridiculous.\n\n<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Velociwolf.label: velociwolf
LanguageData.GR_Velociwolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Velociwolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Velociwolf.tools.2.label: teeth
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggFertilized.description: Fertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a velociwolf.
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggFertilized.label: velociwolf egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggUnfertilized.description: Unfertilized velociwolf egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_VelociwolfEggUnfertilized.label: velociwolf egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_VenomousFangs.description: This hybrid implant substitutes the subject's jaw with a set of venomous fangs. Useful in combat, but not very comfortable.
LanguageData.GR_VenomousFangs.label: human Implant: venomous fangs
LanguageData.GR_Warhead.label: antigrain warhead
LanguageData.GR_Web.label: web
LanguageData.GR_WeirdMilk.description: This strange milk has a sour, chemical taste.
LanguageData.GR_WeirdMilk.label: weird milk
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.description:
Some insane madman thought it would be an excellent idea to strap a flamethrower to a dog and send it out into the world to see what happens. Don’t be surprised to find out this unusual hybrid started a massive forest fire trying to hunt a rabbit.

A canine hybrid with recessive boomalope genes. A bit more dim-witted compared to their baseline, due to a large amount of brain processing power dedicated to the complex internal organs that permit it to spit out burning chemfuel. Excellent flankers thanks to their increased speed and ranged ability.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.label: wolfalope
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfalope.verbs.0.label: fire breath
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.description:
A massively muscled boi with strong claws, sharper teeth and a mean attitude. Adorable when it’s trying to jump you to lick your face, less so when those same paws are trying to restrain you so it can bite your head off.

A canine hybrid with recessive ursine genes. Stronger, more aggressive and with a bigger appetite, this hybrid’s particular lethality comes from its tendency to attack the enemy’s head to try and decapitate them.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. There is a 25% chance that wolfbear attacks will target its victim's head, possibly decapitating enemies. Fun!
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.label: wolfbear
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbear.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.description:
Probably an excellent example of a precursor to the alphabeaver, this beastie has an insane appetite and constantly growing teeth that it wears down by chewing on trees. Either consider creating a tree grove just for them to eat or moving to a jungle because they’re gonna be procreating a whole bunch.

A canine hybrid with recessive rodent genes. Fast gestation period, highly intelligent, incredibly fast and lethal. The price for these benefits is that it has a constant hunger for trees and shares ancestry with another rapid dendrovore.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.label: wolfbeaver
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfbeaver.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.description:
Dogs and cats are domesticated predators who are just the absolute cutest. So someone wanted to see how dangerous they could make something by combining these two proto predators. The results could be imagined but we’ll let them speak for themselves.

A canine hybrid with recessive feline genes. A combination of two predators makes for an incredible loyal killing machine. Quick, lethal, low maintenance and a desire for companionship. If that wasn’t already enough, it can even randomly attack twice by lunging with both claws and teeth.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Like all cat hybrids, they have an improved melee dodge ability and a short nuzzle period. Double slash: 25% of the time its attacks will strike twice.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.label: wolfcat
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfcat.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.description:
Not many people are familiar with just how vicious a chicken can be, with them even going so far to pick at their own wounds or actively eating each other if hungry. Combining that with the sleek hunting form of a wolf and making them vegetarian creates a deadly combo. You’d go mad too if you smelled something delicious but couldn’t stomach eating it.

A canine hybrid with recessive avian genes. Very quick on their feet and very filthy, care must be taken as they are far less suited to cold temperatures. The inherent viciousness of chickens has been combined with a proper combat form, vastly boosting their speed after killing its prey.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards. Blood frenzy ability: after killing prey, the wolfchicken receives 200% speed for an hour.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.label: wolfchicken
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfchicken.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WolfchickenEggFertilized.description: Fertilized Wolfchicken egg. If all goes well, it should hatch into one of these abominations. It can be eaten raw, but it's much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_WolfchickenEggFertilized.label: wolfchicken egg (fert.)
LanguageData.GR_WolfchickenEggUnfertilized.descriptio: Unfertilized Wolfchicken egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked.
LanguageData.GR_WolfchickenEggUnfertilized.label: wolfchicken egg (unfert.)
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.description:
While they make for a completely daft canine hybrid, they’re still smart enough not to wander off. Anyone familiar with dogs that shed enough to make another animal will feel right at home with this hybrid. Even the milk it produces comes already laden with hair for your displeasure!

A canine hybrid with recessive muffalo genes. A great producer of thick wool and, regrettably, hairy milk. This allows them to survive in even more extreme colds, but they frequently make a mess, lack most of their intellect and need to eat a lot.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All canine hybrids get the Adrenaline rush ability, which allows them to move at 200% speed for a short time, but be tired and move slower for half a day afterwards.
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.label: wolffalo
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolffalo.tools.2.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WolffaloMilk.description: This milk seems to be filled with strands of grey wool. Disgusting, and quite hard to drink...
LanguageData.GR_WolffaloMilk.label: hairy milk
LanguageData.GR_Wolfhorse.description:
Combining two of the most renowned endurance hunters in human history has resulted in man’s best friend whom you can also ride into battle with. Needless to say, the creation of this capricious canine was celebrated by many wealthy families who otherwise caused unfortunate accidents when they tried to ride their household dog when they clearly became too old or heavy for them to carry. What was not appreciated was their tendency to overestimate their - admittedly formidable - predatory capabilities.

An equine hybrid with recessive canine genes. A fast and ferocious dedicated carnivore sharing the hyperactive nature of sled dogs. Requires slightly more frequent retraining compared to similar hybrids but is overall better due to not having any extra features.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All equine hybrids are good for caravaning. A caravan with one of them needs a shorter resting period, only stopping to rest between 23:30 PM and 4 AM.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfhorse.label: wolfhorse
LanguageData.GR_Wolfhorse.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_Wolfhorse.tools.1.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfhorse.tools.2.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_WolfJaws.description: An implant that uses genetic splicing with wolf genes to give an old (or damaged) jaw decent functioning. This implant is not meant to replace healthy jaws.
LanguageData.GR_WolfJaws.label: animal Implant: wolf fangs
LanguageData.GR_WolfMuscularFibers.description: The subjects legs can be modified by weaving hybrid human/wolf muscle fibers within the base tissue, giving them a significant strength and speed boost.
LanguageData.GR_WolfMuscularFibers.label: human Implant: wolf muscular fibers
LanguageData.GR_WolfPelt.description: This wolf pelt can be used as a trade good. It can also be crafted into a decorative wolf rug.
LanguageData.GR_WolfPelt.label: wolf pelt
LanguageData.GR_WolfRug.description: A wolf pelt rug. Taxidermy seems to be in vogue on the Rim!
LanguageData.GR_WolfRug.label: wolf rug
LanguageData.GR_Wolfscarab.description:
The answer to all your worries about escaping slaves. These omnivorous vicious beasties are effectively exotic hunting dogs, and the most exotic and forbidden prey of all is humans. That is, provided they’re practically unable to defend themselves. Everyone knows you can kill as many people as you like, just don’t hurt the poor puppy trying to tear your throat out.

An insectoid hybrid with recessive canine genes. Incredibly fast and with multiple hardened jaw plates, these ferocious hunting dogs make for an excellent, intelligent combat hybrid. Unable to take much punishment but the armour plating should help against those lacking in offensive weaponry.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All insectoid hybrids have good armor and no toxic sensitivity, but they are always sterile and can't reproduce, even if a fertility booster is used to create them.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfscarab.label: wolfscarab
LanguageData.GR_Wolfscarab.tools.1.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.description: A terrifyingly alien creature, the wolf shrimp is keenly intelligent like its wolf forefather, slithering in blinding speed towards its prey.\n\nUseful abilities: Extremely fast regenerative capabilities.
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.label: wolf shrimp
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.0.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.2.label: left blade
LanguageData.GR_WolfShrimp.tools.3.label: right blade
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.description:
A great doggo that sheds snakeskin instead of fur, so don’t be too alarmed when scratching at an itchy spot makes skin flake off. It’ll shed itself, even when out hunting prey, so be prepared to occasionally go on a long trek to pick up the valuable skin.

A reptile hybrid with recessive canine genes. Fast, intelligent and with a nasty venomous bite, these hybrids make for an excellent hunting hound. They lack any notion of fear so they may end up going after much larger, more dangerous prey and achieving a pyrrhic victory.

<color=#E5E54C>Gameplay effect:</color> All reptile hybrids have good temperature tolerance ranges and venomous attacks.
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.label: wolfsnake
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_Wolfsnake.tools.3.label: head
LanguageData.GR_WoolyWolf.description: Just like Wooly Mammoths and Wooly Rhinos, the Wooly Wolf is a creature perfectly adapted to cold climates. Wooly wolves are an excellent source of high quality wool, since they produce it much faster than a mammoth.\n\nUseful abilities: Fast wool production
LanguageData.GR_WoolyWolf.label: wooly Wolf
LanguageData.GR_WoolyWolf.tools.0.label: left claw
LanguageData.GR_WoolyWolf.tools.1.label: right claw
LanguageData.GR_WoolyWolf.tools.3.label: tusk
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Cattle_Drone.label: cattle drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Cattle_Queen.label: cattle queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Clucking_Drone.label: clucking drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Clucking_Queen.label: clucking queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Exploding_Drone.label: exploding drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Exploding_Queen.label: exploding queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Horned_Drone.label: horned drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Horned_Queen.label: horned queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hound_Drone.label: hound drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hound_Queen.label: hound queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hybernating_Drone.label: hybernating drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Hybernating_Queen.label: hybernating queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Leather.description: Hard overlapping scales of insect chitin laid over a thick layer of semi-rigid leather. Quite durable in combat, but provides little protection from weather.
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Leather.label: bee leather
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Megaspidery_Drone.label: megaspidery drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Megaspidery_Queen.label: megaspidery queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Mousy_Drone.label: mousy drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Mousy_Queen.label: mousy queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Organic_Drone.label: organic drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Organic_Queen.label: organic queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Purring_Drone.label: purring drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Purring_Queen.label: purring queen
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Scaly_Drone.label: scaly drone
LanguageData.RB_Bee_Scaly_Queen.label: scaly queen
LanguageData.RB_BeeBomb.label: bee shell
LanguageData.RB_BeeEggsActive.description: Fertilized chickenbee egg. It can be eaten raw, but it's much, much better cooked. If all goes well, it should hatch into a chickenbee.
LanguageData.RB_BeeEggsActive.label: chickenbee egg (fert.)
