LanguageData.RB_Cattle_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by cattle bees. Cattle bees have muffalo genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a thick white pelt, and produce milk, wool and muffalo genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get milk, wool and muffalo genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Cattle_Honeycomb.label: cattle honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Clucking_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by clucking bees. Clucking bees have chicken genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in feathers, and produce eggs and avian genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get bee eggs, genewax and avian genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Clucking_Honeycomb.label: clucking honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Exploding_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by exploding bees. Exploding bees have boomalope genes spliced in their genome. This bees produce genetic honey, chemfuel, and boom genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, chemfuel, and booma genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Exploding_Honeycomb.label: exploding honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_GeneticHoney.description: This honey has been extracted from any genetic honeycomb, and has a very strange greenish tone that makes it less appetizing. Decays incredibly fast, but it can be consumed as a potent physically enhancing drug that doesn't cause any side effects. This honey can be consumed raw or cooked with other ingredients, and its properties transfer to the resulting meal.
LanguageData.RB_GeneticHoney.label: genetic honey
LanguageData.RB_Genewax.description: Genewax is extracted from combs of the Genetic family (any Vanilla Genetics Expanded hybrid bees) and is much less resistant than regular beeswax. Its only practical application is that it can be used to create genoframes, instead of corpses.
LanguageData.RB_Genewax.label: genewax
LanguageData.RB_Horned_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by horned bees. Horned bees have thrumbo genes spliced in their genome. They take a very long time to generate honeycombs, but they produce highly sought after colossal genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax, and colossal genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Horned_Honeycomb.label: horned honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Hound_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by hound bees. Hound bees have wolf genes spliced in their genome. They produce canine genetic material. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and canine genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Hound_Honeycomb.label: hound honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Hybernating_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by hybernating bees. Hybernating bees have grizzly bear genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in a soft fur. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and bear genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Hybernating_Honeycomb.label: hybernating honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Megaspidery_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by megaspidery bees. Megaspidery bees have insectoid genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in chitinous plates and produce insect jelly and insectoid genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get insect jelly, genewax and insectoid genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Megaspidery_Honeycomb.label: megaspidery honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Mousy_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by mousy bees. Mousy bees have rodent genes spliced in their genome. They produce rodent genetic materials and many find their constant high-pitched squeaking sounds to be quite distracting. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and rodent genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Mousy_Honeycomb.label: mousy honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Organic_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by organic bees. Organic bees are genetically modified bees that extract cellulose fibers from nearby plants and convert it into a slurry of genetic material which can be refined into template genomes. The main use of these bees, however, is to hybridize them with other bees to create genetic bees of different subspecies. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get honey, beeswax and template genomes.
LanguageData.RB_Organic_Honeycomb.label: organic honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Purring_Honeycomb.description: This is a honeycomb produced by purring bees. Purring bees have cat genes spliced in their genome. They produce feline genetic materials and a lot of videos on the internet. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and feline genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Purring_Honeycomb.label: purring honeycomb
LanguageData.RB_Quill.label: quill
LanguageData.RB_RandomGeneticResearch.label: bee research (unfinished)
LanguageData.RB_Scaly_Honeycomb.description: TScaly bees have reptile genes spliced in their genome. They are covered in tough looking scales and produce reptilian genetic materials. Honeycombs can be procesed in a Honey Extractor to get genetic honey, genewax and reptile genetic material.
LanguageData.RB_Scaly_Honeycomb.label: scaly honeycomb
LanguageData.VCE_DinoCheese.description: A fake cheese, from a fake milk. Best not to think where this came from.
LanguageData.VCE_DinoCheese.label: dino "cheese"
LanguageData.VCE_EldritchCheese.description: Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cheese R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn. The only brand of cheese that would please the Great Old Ones themselves.
LanguageData.VCE_EldritchCheese.label: eldritch cheese
LanguageData.VCE_GreenCheese.description: A green coloured cheese that is actually quite unappetizing.
LanguageData.VCE_GreenCheese.label: green cheese
LanguageData.VCE_ParaceramuffaloBrie.description: A delicious cheese that takes a VERY long time to ripen. Paraceramuffalo Brie is certainly worth the wait, being one of the most nutritive cheeses in the Rim.
LanguageData.VCE_ParaceramuffaloBrie.label: paraceramuffalo brie
